6.1 Heatmap to visualize differentially expressed genes

```{r}

mutant_genotypes <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")
lfc_col_pattern <- "genotype_%s_vs_WT"
pval_col_pattern <- "WaldPvalue_genotype_%s_vs_WT"

lfc_cols <- sapply(mutant_genotypes, function(m) sprintf(lfc_col_pattern, m))
pval_cols <- sapply(mutant_genotypes, function(m) sprintf(pval_col_pattern, m))

# --- Step 2: Extract LFC and P-value data, and adjust P-values ---
if (!all(lfc_cols %in% names(rowData(dds_RNA))) || !all(pval_cols %in% names(rowData(dds_RNA)))) {
  stop("One or more LFC/P-value columns not found in rowData(dds_RNA). Please check names.")
}

lfc_matrix <- as.data.frame(rowData(dds_RNA)[, lfc_cols])
pval_matrix <- as.data.frame(rowData(dds_RNA)[, pval_cols])
rownames(lfc_matrix) <- rownames(dds_RNA)
rownames(pval_matrix) <- rownames(dds_RNA)

padj_matrix <- as.data.frame(sapply(pval_matrix, function(pcol) p.adjust(pcol, method = "BH")))
rownames(padj_matrix) <- rownames(dds_RNA)
colnames(padj_matrix) <- paste0("padj_", mutant_genotypes, "_vs_WT")

# --- Step 3: Iterative Thresholding to find Consistently DE Genes ---
threshold_sequence <- c(0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30)
candidate_gene_names <- character(0)
final_threshold_rna <- NA

message("Starting iterative thresholding to find consistently DE genes...")
for (thresh in threshold_sequence) {
  is_significant_all_conditions <- (rowSums(padj_matrix < thresh, na.rm = TRUE) == length(mutant_genotypes))
  current_candidates <- rownames(padj_matrix)[is_significant_all_conditions]
  
  message(sprintf("With padj < %.2f, found %d genes DE in all %d mutant conditions vs WT.",
                  thresh, length(current_candidates), length(mutant_genotypes)))
                  
  if (length(current_candidates) >= 25) { # Keeping the pool target at 25 is still reasonable
    candidate_gene_names <- current_candidates
    final_threshold_rna <- thresh
    message(sprintf("Sufficient candidates (%d) found at padj < %.2f.", length(candidate_gene_names), final_threshold_rna))
    break
  }
  if (thresh == tail(threshold_sequence, 1) && length(current_candidates) > 0 && length(candidate_gene_names) == 0) {
      candidate_gene_names <- current_candidates
      final_threshold_rna <- thresh
      message(sprintf("Proceeding with %d candidates found at the highest threshold padj < %.2f.", length(candidate_gene_names), final_threshold_rna))
  }
}

if (length(candidate_gene_names) == 0) {
  stop("Could not find any genes consistently DE across all conditions. Cannot proceed.")
}

# --- Step 4: Calculate Average LFC for Candidate Genes ---
candidate_lfc_matrix <- lfc_matrix[candidate_gene_names, , drop = FALSE]
avg_lfc <- rowMeans(candidate_lfc_matrix, na.rm = TRUE)

candidate_df_rna <- data.frame(
  gene = candidate_gene_names,
  avg_lfc = avg_lfc
)

# --- Step 5: Select Top 15 Up and Top 15 Down-regulated Genes ---
# CHANGE: Updated from 50 to 15
up_regulated_rna <- candidate_df_rna %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_lfc > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_lfc)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

down_regulated_rna <- candidate_df_rna %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_lfc < 0) %>%
  arrange(avg_lfc) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

final_gene_list_rna <- unique(c(up_regulated_rna$gene, down_regulated_rna$gene))

message(sprintf("Selected %d unique genes for the RNA heatmap (%d up, %d down).",
                length(final_gene_list_rna), nrow(up_regulated_rna), nrow(down_regulated_rna)))



# Ensure 'final_gene_list_rna' and 'dds_RNA' are available from Phase 1.
if (length(final_gene_list_rna) > 0) {
  # --- Step 6: Get VST-transformed expression matrix ---
  message("Performing Variance Stabilizing Transformation...")
  vsd_rna <- vst(dds_RNA, blind = FALSE)
  vst_matrix_rna <- assay(vsd_rna)

  # --- Step 7: Subset VST matrix to selected genes ---
  heatmap_matrix_rna_unscaled <- vst_matrix_rna[final_gene_list_rna, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # --- Step 8: Calculate Z-scores PER BACKGROUND 
  message("Calculating Z-scores separately for each background...")
  
  # Identify columns for each background
  col_data <- as.data.frame(colData(dds_RNA))
  mgh_cols <- rownames(col_data[col_data$background == "MGH", ])
  b8330_cols <- rownames(col_data[col_data$background == "8330", ])
  
  # Subset the unscaled matrix by background
  mgh_matrix_unscaled <- heatmap_matrix_rna_unscaled[, mgh_cols, drop = FALSE]
  b8330_matrix_unscaled <- heatmap_matrix_rna_unscaled[, b8330_cols, drop = FALSE]

  calculate_z_score <- function(mat) {
    # Initialize a matrix for the results, filled with 0s
    mat_scaled <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(mat), ncol = ncol(mat), dimnames = dimnames(mat))
    
    # Identify rows (genes) that have variance
    variances <- apply(mat, 1, var, na.rm=TRUE)
    has_variance <- variances > 1e-6
    
    # Only proceed if there are any rows with variance
    if(any(has_variance)) {
      # Subset the matrix to only include rows with variance
      mat_subset <- mat[has_variance, , drop = FALSE]
      
      # Scale the subset and transpose back
      scaled_subset <- t(scale(t(mat_subset), center = TRUE, scale = TRUE))
      
      # Place the scaled values back into our results matrix
      mat_scaled[has_variance, ] <- scaled_subset
    }
    
    return(mat_scaled)
  }

  z_mgh <- calculate_z_score(mgh_matrix_unscaled)
  z_8330 <- calculate_z_score(b8330_matrix_unscaled)
  
  # Combine the Z-score matrices and restore original column order
  z_score_matrix_combined <- cbind(z_mgh, z_8330)
  z_score_matrix_final <- z_score_matrix_combined[, colnames(heatmap_matrix_rna_unscaled)]
  
  # --- Step 9: Cap Z-scores to the range [-2, 2] ---
  message("Capping Z-scores to a range of -2 to 2.")
  z_score_matrix_final[z_score_matrix_final > 2] <- 2
  z_score_matrix_final[z_score_matrix_final < -2] <- -2
  
  # --- Step 10: Create Column Annotations and Colors ---
  col_annotation_rna <- as.data.frame(colData(dds_RNA)[, c("background", "genotype")])
  
  gg_color_hue <- function(n) {
    hues = seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
    hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100)[1:n]
  }
  
  genotype_levels <- levels(col_annotation_rna$genotype)
  genotype_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(genotype_levels)), genotype_levels)
  
  background_levels <- levels(col_annotation_rna$background)
  background_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(background_levels)), background_levels)
  
  ann_colors_rna <- list(
    genotype = genotype_colors,
    background = background_colors
  )
    
  # --- Step 11: Generate Heatmap ---
  message("Generating RNA DEG heatmap...")
    
  pheatmap_rna <- pheatmap(
  z_score_matrix_final,
  annotation_col = col_annotation_rna,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors_rna,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 10, name = "RdBu")))(100),
  main = sprintf("Heatmap of Top DEGs (Z-scores capped at +/-2)\n(Top 15 Up/Down, DE at padj < %.2f)", final_threshold_rna),
  scale = "none",
  breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  legend_breaks = c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2),
  border_color = NA # <-- This line removes the grid
)
    
print(pheatmap_rna)
message("RNA DEG heatmap generation complete.")
  
} else {
  message("Final gene list for RNA heatmap is empty after selection. Cannot generate RNA heatmap.")
}
```

6.2 Heatmap to visualize differentially expressed proteins

```{r}
# --- Step 1: Define parameters and load results data ---
# Assume 'dep_MGH_results_new' and 'dep_8330_results_new' are loaded DFrame objects

data_imp_MGH_new <- readRDS(file = "./data_imp_MGH_BH_new.rds")
dep_MGH_results_new <- readRDS(file = "./dep_MGH_BH_new.rds")

data_imp_8330_new <- readRDS(file = "./data_imp_8330_BH_new.rds")
dep_8330_results_new <- readRDS(file = "./dep_8330_BH_new.rds")

mutant_genotypes <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")

# --- Step 2: Extract and Align LFC and P-value Data for Both Backgrounds ---
message("Extracting and aligning proteomics results from MGH and 8330...")

# Function to extract specific columns (LFC or padj) for the 6 comparisons
extract_dep_data <- function(dep_results_df, col_pattern) {
  cols_to_get <- sapply(mutant_genotypes, function(m) sprintf(col_pattern, m))
  
  if (!all(cols_to_get %in% names(dep_results_df))) {
    stop(paste("One or more columns not found. Pattern:", col_pattern))
  }
  
  # Ensure rownames are set (e.g., using gene symbols)
  extracted_df <- as.data.frame(dep_results_df[, cols_to_get])
  rownames(extracted_df) <- rownames(dep_results_df)
  return(extracted_df)
}

# Define the patterns for LFC (diff) and adjusted p-value columns
lfc_pattern <- "%s_vs_WT_diff"
padj_pattern <- "%s_vs_WT_p.adj"

# Extract the data for each background from the correct slot
lfc_mgh <- extract_dep_data(rowData(dep_MGH_results_new), lfc_pattern)
padj_mgh <- extract_dep_data(rowData(dep_MGH_results_new), padj_pattern)

lfc_8330 <- extract_dep_data(rowData(dep_8330_results_new), lfc_pattern)
padj_8330 <- extract_dep_data(rowData(dep_8330_results_new), padj_pattern)


# Find common proteins between the two result sets
common_proteins <- intersect(rownames(lfc_mgh), rownames(lfc_8330))
message(sprintf("Found %d common proteins between the MGH and 8330 result sets.", length(common_proteins)))

# Align all data frames to these common proteins
lfc_mgh <- lfc_mgh[common_proteins, ]
padj_mgh <- padj_mgh[common_proteins, ]
lfc_8330 <- lfc_8330[common_proteins, ]
padj_8330 <- padj_8330[common_proteins, ]

# --- Step 3: Iterative Thresholding to find Consistently DE Proteins ---
threshold_sequence <- c(0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30)
candidate_protein_names <- character(0)
final_threshold_protein <- NA

message("Starting iterative thresholding to find consistently DE proteins...")
for (thresh in threshold_sequence) {
  
  # For each mutant genotype, check if protein is significant in MGH OR 8330
  sig_per_genotype <- sapply(1:length(mutant_genotypes), function(i) {
    (padj_mgh[, i] < thresh) | (padj_8330[, i] < thresh)
  })
  
  # A protein is a candidate if it was significant for ALL 6 genotypes
  is_candidate <- (rowSums(sig_per_genotype, na.rm = TRUE) == length(mutant_genotypes))
  current_candidates <- common_proteins[is_candidate]
  
  message(sprintf("With padj < %.2f, found %d proteins DE in all %d mutant types (in at least one background).",
                  thresh, length(current_candidates), length(mutant_genotypes)))
  
  if (length(current_candidates) >= 25) {
    candidate_protein_names <- current_candidates
    final_threshold_protein <- thresh
    message(sprintf("Sufficient candidates (%d) found at padj < %.2f.", length(candidate_protein_names), final_threshold_protein))
    break
  }
  if (thresh == tail(threshold_sequence, 1) && length(current_candidates) > 0 && length(candidate_protein_names) == 0) {
    candidate_protein_names <- current_candidates
    final_threshold_protein <- thresh
    message(sprintf("Proceeding with %d candidates found at the highest threshold padj < %.2f.", length(candidate_protein_names), final_threshold_protein))
  }
}

if (length(candidate_protein_names) == 0) {
  stop("Could not find any proteins consistently DE across all conditions. Cannot proceed.")
}

# --- Step 4: Calculate Average LFC for Candidate Proteins using the specified logic ---
message("Calculating representative LFCs for ranking...")

# Subset all data to only the candidate proteins
lfc_mgh_cand <- lfc_mgh[candidate_protein_names, ]
padj_mgh_cand <- padj_mgh[candidate_protein_names, ]
lfc_8330_cand <- lfc_8330[candidate_protein_names, ]
padj_8330_cand <- padj_8330[candidate_protein_names, ]

# Calculate the 6 representative LFCs for each protein
representative_lfcs <- sapply(1:length(mutant_genotypes), function(i) {
  sig_mgh <- padj_mgh_cand[, i] < final_threshold_protein
  sig_8330 <- padj_8330_cand[, i] < final_threshold_protein
  
  lfc1 <- lfc_mgh_cand[, i]
  lfc2 <- lfc_8330_cand[, i]
  
  # Apply ranking logic
  ifelse(sig_mgh & sig_8330, (lfc1 + lfc2) / 2,
         ifelse(sig_mgh, lfc1, lfc2))
})

# Calculate the final ranking score (average of the 6 representative LFCs)
avg_lfc <- rowMeans(representative_lfcs, na.rm = TRUE)

candidate_df_protein <- data.frame(
  protein = candidate_protein_names,
  avg_lfc = avg_lfc
)

# --- Step 5: Select Top 15 Up and Top 15 Down-regulated Proteins ---
up_regulated_protein <- candidate_df_protein %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_lfc > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_lfc)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

down_regulated_protein <- candidate_df_protein %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_lfc < 0) %>%
  arrange(avg_lfc) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

final_protein_list <- unique(c(up_regulated_protein$protein, down_regulated_protein$protein))

message(sprintf("Selected %d unique proteins for the heatmap (%d up, %d down).",
                length(final_protein_list), nrow(up_regulated_protein), nrow(down_regulated_protein)))

# --- HEATMAP GENERATION ---
if (length(final_protein_list) > 0) {
  
  # --- Step 6: Combine Expression Data from MGH and 8330 (with fix) ---
  message("Combining MGH and 8330 proteomics expression data...")
  
  # Extract assay data and colData
  exp_mgh <- assay(data_imp_MGH_new)
  col_data_mgh <- as.data.frame(colData(data_imp_MGH_new))
  
  exp_8330 <- assay(data_imp_8330_new)
  col_data_8330 <- as.data.frame(colData(data_imp_8330_new))
  
  # *** FIX: Create unique sample names by prefixing with background ***
  colnames(exp_mgh) <- paste0("MGH_", colnames(exp_mgh))
  rownames(col_data_mgh) <- paste0("MGH_", rownames(col_data_mgh))
  
  colnames(exp_8330) <- paste0("B8330_", colnames(exp_8330))
  rownames(col_data_8330) <- paste0("B8330_", rownames(col_data_8330))
  
  # Align expression matrices to common proteins
  common_exp_proteins <- intersect(rownames(exp_mgh), rownames(exp_8330))
  exp_mgh <- exp_mgh[common_exp_proteins, ]
  exp_8330 <- exp_8330[common_exp_proteins, ]
  
  # Combine expression matrices and column data (now with unique names)
  combined_exp_matrix <- cbind(exp_mgh, exp_8330)
  combined_col_data <- rbind(col_data_mgh, col_data_8330)
  
  # --- Step 7: Subset to selected proteins ---
  heatmap_matrix_protein_unscaled <- combined_exp_matrix[final_protein_list, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # --- Step 8: Calculate Z-scores PER BACKGROUND ---
  message("Calculating Z-scores separately for each background...")
  
  # Identify columns for each background using the now-unique names
  mgh_cols <- rownames(combined_col_data[combined_col_data$background == "MG", ])
  b8330_cols <- rownames(combined_col_data[combined_col_data$background == "GM", ])
  
  # This subsetting will now work correctly
  mgh_matrix_unscaled <- heatmap_matrix_protein_unscaled[, mgh_cols, drop = FALSE]
  b8330_matrix_unscaled <- heatmap_matrix_protein_unscaled[, b8330_cols, drop = FALSE]
  
  calculate_z_score <- function(mat) {
    mat_scaled <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(mat), ncol = ncol(mat), dimnames = dimnames(mat))
    variances <- apply(mat, 1, var, na.rm=TRUE)
    has_variance <- variances > 1e-6
    if(any(has_variance)) {
      mat_subset <- mat[has_variance, , drop = FALSE]
      scaled_subset <- t(scale(t(mat_subset), center = TRUE, scale = TRUE))
      mat_scaled[has_variance, ] <- scaled_subset
    }
    return(mat_scaled)
  }
  
  z_mgh <- calculate_z_score(mgh_matrix_unscaled)
  z_8330 <- calculate_z_score(b8330_matrix_unscaled)
  
  z_score_matrix_combined <- cbind(z_mgh, z_8330)
  z_score_matrix_final <- z_score_matrix_combined[, colnames(heatmap_matrix_protein_unscaled)]
  
  # --- Step 9: Cap Z-scores to the range [-2, 2] ---
  message("Capping Z-scores to a range of -2 to 2.")
  z_score_matrix_final[z_score_matrix_final > 2] <- 2
  z_score_matrix_final[z_score_matrix_final < -2] <- -2
  
  # --- Step 10: Create Column Annotations and Colors ---
  combined_col_data$background[combined_col_data$background == "MG"] <- "MGH"
  combined_col_data$background[combined_col_data$background == "GM"] <- "8330"
  col_annotation_protein <- combined_col_data[, c("background", "condition")]
  colnames(col_annotation_protein)[2] <- "genotype"
  
  gg_color_hue <- function(n) {
    hues = seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
    hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100)[1:n]
  }
  
  genotype_levels <- levels(as.factor(col_annotation_protein$genotype))
  genotype_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(genotype_levels)), genotype_levels)
  
  background_levels <- levels(as.factor(col_annotation_protein$background))
  background_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(background_levels)), background_levels)
  
  ann_colors_protein <- list(
    genotype = genotype_colors,
    background = background_colors
  )
  
  # --- Step 11: Generate Heatmap ---
message("Generating Protein DEP heatmap...")
pheatmap_protein <- pheatmap(
  z_score_matrix_final,
  annotation_col = col_annotation_protein,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors_protein,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 10, name = "RdBu")))(100),
  main = sprintf("Heatmap of Top DEPs (Z-scores capped at +/-2)\n(Top 15 Up/Down, DE at padj < %.2f)", final_threshold_protein),
  scale = "none",
  breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  legend_breaks = c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2),
  border_color = NA # <--- ADDED THIS LINE
)

print(pheatmap_protein)
message("Protein DEP heatmap generation complete.")
  
} else {
  message("Final protein list for heatmap is empty after selection. Cannot generate protein heatmap.")
}
```

6.3 Heatmap of top dysregulated Ubiquitinated Proteins

```{r}
Ubi_data_imp_MGH_new <- readRDS(file = "./Ubi_data_imp_MGH_BH_new.rds")
deu_MGH_results_new <- readRDS(file = "./Ubi_deu_MGH_BH_new.rds")

Ubi_data_imp_8330_new <- readRDS(file = "./Ubi_data_imp_8330_BH_new.rds")
deu_8330_results_new <- readRDS(file = "./Ubi_deu_8330_BH_new.rds")

mutant_genotypes <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")

# --- Step 2: Extract and Align LFC and P-value Data for Both Backgrounds ---
message("Extracting and aligning proteomics results from MGH and 8330...")

# Function to extract specific columns (LFC or padj) for the 6 comparisons
extract_dep_data <- function(dep_results_df, col_pattern) {
  cols_to_get <- sapply(mutant_genotypes, function(m) sprintf(col_pattern, m))
  
  if (!all(cols_to_get %in% names(dep_results_df))) {
    stop(paste("One or more columns not found. Pattern:", col_pattern))
  }
  
  # Ensure rownames are set (e.g., using gene symbols)
  extracted_df <- as.data.frame(dep_results_df[, cols_to_get])
  rownames(extracted_df) <- rownames(dep_results_df)
  return(extracted_df)
}

# Define the patterns for LFC (diff) and adjusted p-value columns
lfc_pattern <- "%s_vs_WT_diff"
padj_pattern <- "%s_vs_WT_p.adj"

lfc_mgh <- extract_dep_data(rowData(deu_MGH_results_new), lfc_pattern)
padj_mgh <- extract_dep_data(rowData(deu_MGH_results_new), padj_pattern)

padj_8330 <- extract_dep_data(rowData(deu_8330_results_new), padj_pattern)


# Find common proteins between the two result sets
common_proteins <- intersect(rownames(lfc_mgh), rownames(lfc_8330))
message(sprintf("Found %d common proteins between the MGH and 8330 result sets.", length(common_proteins)))

# Align all data frames to these common proteins
lfc_mgh <- lfc_mgh[common_proteins, ]
padj_mgh <- padj_mgh[common_proteins, ]
lfc_8330 <- lfc_8330[common_proteins, ]
padj_8330 <- padj_8330[common_proteins, ]

# --- Step 3: Iterative Thresholding to find Consistently DE Proteins ---
threshold_sequence <- c(0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30)
candidate_protein_names <- character(0)
final_threshold_protein <- NA

message("Starting iterative thresholding to find consistently DE proteins...")
for (thresh in threshold_sequence) {
  
  # For each mutant genotype, check if protein is significant in MGH OR 8330
  sig_per_genotype <- sapply(1:length(mutant_genotypes), function(i) {
    (padj_mgh[, i] < thresh) | (padj_8330[, i] < thresh)
  })
  
  # A protein is a candidate if it was significant for ALL 6 genotypes
  is_candidate <- (rowSums(sig_per_genotype, na.rm = TRUE) == length(mutant_genotypes))
  current_candidates <- common_proteins[is_candidate]
  
  message(sprintf("With padj < %.2f, found %d proteins DE in all %d mutant types (in at least one background).",
                  thresh, length(current_candidates), length(mutant_genotypes)))
  
  if (length(current_candidates) >= 25) {
    candidate_protein_names <- current_candidates
    final_threshold_protein <- thresh
    message(sprintf("Sufficient candidates (%d) found at padj < %.2f.", length(candidate_protein_names), final_threshold_protein))
    break
  }
  if (thresh == tail(threshold_sequence, 1) && length(current_candidates) > 0 && length(candidate_protein_names) == 0) {
    candidate_protein_names <- current_candidates
    final_threshold_protein <- thresh
    message(sprintf("Proceeding with %d candidates found at the highest threshold padj < %.2f.", length(candidate_protein_names), final_threshold_protein))
  }
}

if (length(candidate_protein_names) == 0) {
  stop("Could not find any proteins consistently DE across all conditions. Cannot proceed.")
}

# --- Step 4: Calculate Average LFC for Candidate Proteins using the specified logic ---
message("Calculating representative LFCs for ranking...")

# Subset all data to only the candidate proteins
lfc_mgh_cand <- lfc_mgh[candidate_protein_names, ]
padj_mgh_cand <- padj_mgh[candidate_protein_names, ]
lfc_8330_cand <- lfc_8330[candidate_protein_names, ]
padj_8330_cand <- padj_8330[candidate_protein_names, ]

# Calculate the 6 representative LFCs for each protein
representative_lfcs <- sapply(1:length(mutant_genotypes), function(i) {
  sig_mgh <- padj_mgh_cand[, i] < final_threshold_protein
  sig_8330 <- padj_8330_cand[, i] < final_threshold_protein
  
  lfc1 <- lfc_mgh_cand[, i]
  lfc2 <- lfc_8330_cand[, i]
  
  # Apply ranking logic
  ifelse(sig_mgh & sig_8330, (lfc1 + lfc2) / 2,
         ifelse(sig_mgh, lfc1, lfc2))
})

# Calculate the final ranking score (average of the 6 representative LFCs)
avg_lfc <- rowMeans(representative_lfcs, na.rm = TRUE)

candidate_df_protein <- data.frame(
  protein = candidate_protein_names,
  avg_lfc = avg_lfc
)

# --- Step 5: Select Top 15 Up and Top 15 Down-regulated Proteins ---
up_regulated_protein <- candidate_df_protein %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_lfc > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_lfc)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

down_regulated_protein <- candidate_df_protein %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_lfc < 0) %>%
  arrange(avg_lfc) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

final_protein_list <- unique(c(up_regulated_protein$protein, down_regulated_protein$protein))

message(sprintf("Selected %d unique proteins for the heatmap (%d up, %d down).",
                length(final_protein_list), nrow(up_regulated_protein), nrow(down_regulated_protein)))

# --- HEATMAP GENERATION ---
if (length(final_protein_list) > 0) {
  
  # --- Step 6: Combine Expression Data from MGH and 8330 (with fix) ---
  message("Combining MGH and 8330 proteomics expression data...")
  
  # Extract assay data and colData
  exp_mgh <- assay(Ubi_data_imp_MGH_new)
  col_data_mgh <- as.data.frame(colData(Ubi_data_imp_MGH_new))
  
  exp_8330 <- assay(Ubi_data_imp_8330_new)
  col_data_8330 <- as.data.frame(colData(Ubi_data_imp_8330_new))
  
  # *** FIX: Create unique sample names by prefixing with background ***
  colnames(exp_mgh) <- paste0("MGH_", colnames(exp_mgh))
  rownames(col_data_mgh) <- paste0("MGH_", rownames(col_data_mgh))
  
  colnames(exp_8330) <- paste0("B8330_", colnames(exp_8330))
  rownames(col_data_8330) <- paste0("B8330_", rownames(col_data_8330))
  
  # Align expression matrices to common proteins
  common_exp_proteins <- intersect(rownames(exp_mgh), rownames(exp_8330))
  exp_mgh <- exp_mgh[common_exp_proteins, ]
  exp_8330 <- exp_8330[common_exp_proteins, ]
  
  # Combine expression matrices and column data (now with unique names)
  combined_exp_matrix <- cbind(exp_mgh, exp_8330)
  combined_col_data <- rbind(col_data_mgh, col_data_8330)
  
  # --- Step 7: Subset to selected proteins ---
  heatmap_matrix_protein_unscaled <- combined_exp_matrix[final_protein_list, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # --- Step 8: Calculate Z-scores PER BACKGROUND ---
  message("Calculating Z-scores separately for each background...")
  
  # Identify columns for each background using the now-unique names
  mgh_cols <- rownames(combined_col_data[combined_col_data$background == "MG", ])
  b8330_cols <- rownames(combined_col_data[combined_col_data$background == "GM", ])
  
  mgh_matrix_unscaled <- heatmap_matrix_protein_unscaled[, mgh_cols, drop = FALSE]
  b8330_matrix_unscaled <- heatmap_matrix_protein_unscaled[, b8330_cols, drop = FALSE]
  
  calculate_z_score <- function(mat) {
    mat_scaled <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(mat), ncol = ncol(mat), dimnames = dimnames(mat))
    variances <- apply(mat, 1, var, na.rm=TRUE)
    has_variance <- variances > 1e-6
    if(any(has_variance)) {
      mat_subset <- mat[has_variance, , drop = FALSE]
      scaled_subset <- t(scale(t(mat_subset), center = TRUE, scale = TRUE))
      mat_scaled[has_variance, ] <- scaled_subset
    }
    return(mat_scaled)
  }
  
  z_mgh <- calculate_z_score(mgh_matrix_unscaled)
  z_8330 <- calculate_z_score(b8330_matrix_unscaled)
  
  z_score_matrix_combined <- cbind(z_mgh, z_8330)
  z_score_matrix_final <- z_score_matrix_combined[, colnames(heatmap_matrix_protein_unscaled)]
  
  # --- Step 9: Cap Z-scores to the range [-2, 2] ---
  message("Capping Z-scores to a range of -2 to 2.")
  z_score_matrix_final[z_score_matrix_final > 2] <- 2
  z_score_matrix_final[z_score_matrix_final < -2] <- -2
  
  # --- Step 10: Create Column Annotations and Colors ---
  combined_col_data$background[combined_col_data$background == "MG"] <- "MGH"
  combined_col_data$background[combined_col_data$background == "GM"] <- "8330"
  col_annotation_protein <- combined_col_data[, c("background", "condition")]
  colnames(col_annotation_protein)[2] <- "genotype"
  
  gg_color_hue <- function(n) {
    hues = seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
    hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100)[1:n]
  }
  
  genotype_levels <- levels(as.factor(col_annotation_protein$genotype))
  genotype_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(genotype_levels)), genotype_levels)
  
  background_levels <- levels(as.factor(col_annotation_protein$background))
  background_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(background_levels)), background_levels)
  
  ann_colors_protein <- list(
    genotype = genotype_colors,
    background = background_colors
  )
  
  # --- Step 11: Generate Heatmap ---
message("Generating Protein DEP heatmap...")
pheatmap_ubi <- pheatmap(
  z_score_matrix_final,
  annotation_col = col_annotation_protein,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors_protein,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 10, name = "RdBu")))(100),
  main = sprintf("Heatmap of Top DEPs (Z-scores capped at +/-2)\n(Top 15 Up/Down, DE at padj < %.2f)", final_threshold_protein),
  scale = "none",
  breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  legend_breaks = c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2),
  border_color = NA # <--- ADDED THIS LINE
)

print(pheatmap_ubi)
message("Protein DEP heatmap generation complete.")
  
} else {
  message("Final protein list for heatmap is empty after selection. Cannot generate protein heatmap.")
}
```

7.1 Upset Plots - GO terms - RNA only - Preparation

```{r}
intersectionsOI_GO_onlyRNA <-
  list(
    #single
    "GO_DEG_DelC", 
    "GO_DEG_DupC", 
    "GO_DEG_HetDel",
    "GO_DEG_HomoDel", 
    "GO_DEG_PWSTI", 
    "GO_DEG_PWSTII",

    #SYS-specific RNA
    c("GO_DEG_DelC", "GO_DEG_DupC"),

    #MAGEL2-del specific RNA
    c("GO_DEG_HetDel", "GO_DEG_HomoDel"),

    #PWS-specific RNA
    c("GO_DEG_PWSTI", "GO_DEG_PWSTII"),

    #deletion-specific RNA
    c("GO_DEG_HetDel", "GO_DEG_HomoDel",
         "GO_DEG_PWSTI", "GO_DEG_PWSTII"),

    #all RNA without DelC
    c("GO_DEG_DupC",
         "GO_DEG_HetDel","GO_DEG_HomoDel",
         "GO_DEG_PWSTI","GO_DEG_PWSTII"),

    #all RNA
    c("GO_DEG_DelC","GO_DEG_DupC",
         "GO_DEG_HetDel","GO_DEG_HomoDel",
         "GO_DEG_PWSTI","GO_DEG_PWSTII")

    )
```

7.2 Upset Plots - GO terms - RNA only - Upregulated

```{r}
my_corrected_theme <- list(
  'intersections_matrix' = theme(
    # Fixes the 'legend.margin' warning
    legend.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 5, b = 5, l = 5, unit = 'pt')
    # Add other customizations for the matrix here if needed
    # e.g., axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
  ),
  'overall_sizes' = theme(
    # Add customizations for the set size bars here
    # e.g., axis.title.x = element_text(face = 'bold')
  ),
  # Add theme definitions for other plot components if you customize them
  'selected_theme' = theme() 
)

GO_upregulated_RNA_overlap <- list(
  GO_MGH_DEG_DelC = GO_list_MGH_RNA_up_terms$RNA_DelC_up,
  GO_8330_DEG_DelC = GO_list_8330_RNA_up_terms$RNA_DelC_up,
  GO_MGH_DEG_DupC = GO_list_MGH_RNA_up_terms$RNA_DupC_up,
  GO_8330_DEG_DupC = GO_list_8330_RNA_up_terms$RNA_DupC_up,
  GO_MGH_DEG_HetDel = GO_list_MGH_RNA_up_terms$RNA_HetDel_up,
  GO_8330_DEG_HetDel = GO_list_8330_RNA_up_terms$RNA_HetDel_up,
  GO_MGH_DEG_HomoDel = GO_list_MGH_RNA_up_terms$RNA_HomoDel_up,
  GO_8330_DEG_HomoDel = GO_list_8330_RNA_up_terms$RNA_HomoDel_up,
  GO_MGH_DEG_PWSTI = GO_list_MGH_RNA_up_terms$RNA_PWSTI_up,
  GO_8330_DEG_PWSTI = GO_list_8330_RNA_up_terms$RNA_PWSTI_up,

  tidyr::unite(col = "intersection", -c("GO"), sep = "")

GO_upregulated_onlyRNA_df_with_intersection <-
  GO_upregulated_onlyRNA_df_with_intersection %>%
```

```{r}
intersectionsOI_GO_onlyProtein <-
  list(
    #single
    "GO_DEP_DelC", 
    "GO_DEP_DupC", 
    "GO_DEP_HetDel",
    "GO_DEP_HomoDel", 
    "GO_DEP_PWSTI", 
    "GO_DEP_PWSTII",

    #SYS-specific Protein
    c("GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC"),

    #MAGEL2-del specific Protein
    c("GO_DEP_HetDel", "GO_DEP_HomoDel"),

    #PWS-specific Protein
    c("GO_DEP_PWSTI", "GO_DEP_PWSTII"),

    #deletion-specific Protein
    c("GO_DEP_HetDel", "GO_DEP_HomoDel",
         "GO_DEP_PWSTI", "GO_DEP_PWSTII"),

    #all Protein without DelC
    c("GO_DEP_DupC",
         "GO_DEP_HetDel","GO_DEP_HomoDel",
         "GO_DEP_PWSTI","GO_DEP_PWSTII"),

    #all Protein
    c("GO_DEP_DelC","GO_DEP_DupC",
         "GO_DEP_HetDel","GO_DEP_HomoDel",
         "GO_DEP_PWSTI","GO_DEP_PWSTII")

    )
```

```{r}
         "GO_DEU_PWSTI","GO_DEU_PWSTII"),

    #all Ubi
    c("GO_DEU_DelC","GO_DEU_DupC",
         "GO_DEU_HomoDel",
         "GO_DEU_PWSTI","GO_DEU_PWSTII")

    )

```

```{r}
  "GO_overlap_DEU_PWSTII" = intersect(GO_upregulated_Ubi_overlap$GO_MGH_DEU_PWSTII,

)

  GO_DEU_DupC = GO_list_Ubi_up_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEU_DupC,

#UpSet plot

upset(GO_upregulated_complex_onlyUbi,
      colnames(GO_upregulated_complex_onlyUbi),

      sort_intersections=FALSE,

      queries=list(
          upset_query(intersect=intersectionsOI_GO_onlyUbi[[7]], color=gg_color_hue(4)[4]),
          upset_query(intersect=intersectionsOI_GO_onlyUbi[[8]], color=gg_color_hue(4)[9]),
          upset_query(intersect=intersectionsOI_GO_onlyUbi[[9]], color=gg_color_hue(4)[9]),

      ))


#Query intersections of interest

GO_upregulated_onlyUbi_df <- fromList(GO_upregulated_onlyUbi)
GO_upregulated_onlyUbi_df$GO <- unique(unlist(GO_upregulated_onlyUbi))

GO_upregulated_onlyUbi_df_with_intersection <- GO_upregulated_onlyUbi_df %>%
  tidyr::unite(col = "intersection", -c("GO"), sep = "")

GO_upregulated_onlyUbi_df_with_intersection <-
  GO_upregulated_onlyUbi_df_with_intersection %>%
  group_by(intersection) %>%
  summarise(list = list(GO)) %>%
  mutate(list = setNames(list, intersection)) %>%
```

```{r}
GO_downregulated_RNA_prot_ubi <- list(
  # DEG (Differentially Expressed Genes)
  GO_DEG_DelC = GO_list_RNA_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEG_DelC,
  GO_DEG_DupC = GO_list_RNA_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEG_DupC,
  GO_DEG_HetDel = GO_list_RNA_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEG_HetDel,
  GO_DEG_HomoDel = GO_list_RNA_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEG_HomoDel,
  GO_DEG_PWSTI = GO_list_RNA_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEG_PWSTI,
  GO_DEG_PWSTII = GO_list_RNA_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEG_PWSTII,
  
  # DEP (Differentially Expressed Proteins)
  GO_DEP_DelC = GO_list_Protein_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEP_DelC,
  GO_DEP_DupC = GO_list_Protein_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEP_DupC,
  GO_DEP_HetDel = GO_list_Protein_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEP_HetDel,
  GO_DEP_HomoDel = GO_list_Protein_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEP_HomoDel,
  GO_DEP_PWSTI = GO_list_Protein_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEP_PWSTI,
  GO_DEP_PWSTII = GO_list_Protein_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEP_PWSTII,
  
  # DEU (Differentially Expressed Ubiquitinated Proteins)
  GO_DEU_DelC = GO_list_Ubi_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEU_DelC,
  GO_DEU_DupC = GO_list_Ubi_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEU_DupC,
  GO_DEU_HomoDel = GO_list_Ubi_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEU_HomoDel,
  GO_DEU_PWSTI = GO_list_Ubi_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEU_PWSTI,
  GO_DEU_PWSTII = GO_list_Ubi_down_overlap_terms$GO_overlap_DEU_PWSTII
)

# Create the UpSet plot
UpSetR::upset(
  fromList(GO_downregulated_RNA_prot_ubi), 
  nsets = length(GO_downregulated_RNA_prot_ubi), # Specify the number of sets
  keep.order = TRUE, 
  order.by = "freq",
  sets.bar.color = "black",
  matrix.color = "black",
  mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size",
  sets.x.label = "Set Size"
)


# 3. Define Intersections - DO NOT CHANGE
intersectionsGO_RNA_Prot_Ubi <-
    list(
        #all RNA exluding DelC
        c("GO_DEG_DupC", "GO_DEG_HetDel", "GO_DEG_HomoDel", "GO_DEG_PWSTI", "GO_DEG_PWSTII"),

        # all RNA
        c("GO_DEG_DelC", "GO_DEG_DupC", "GO_DEG_HetDel", "GO_DEG_HomoDel", "GO_DEG_PWSTI", "GO_DEG_PWSTII"),

        # all Prot
        c("GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC", "GO_DEP_HetDel", "GO_DEP_HomoDel", "GO_DEP_PWSTI", "GO_DEP_PWSTII"),

        # all Ubi
        c("GO_DEU_DelC", "GO_DEU_DupC", "GO_DEU_HomoDel", "GO_DEU_PWSTI", "GO_DEU_PWSTII"),

        #SYS-specific RNA
        #c("GO_DEG_DelC", "GO_DEG_DupC"),

        #SYS-specific protein
        #c("GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC"),

        #DelC-specific RNA
        #c("GO_DEG_DelC"),

        #DelC-specific protein
        #c("GO_DEP_DelC"),

        #DupC-specific RNA
        #c("GO_DEG_DupC"),

        #DupC-specific protein
        #c("GO_DEP_DupC"),

        # PWS-specific RNA
        #c("GO_DEG_PWSTI", "GO_DEG_PWSTII"),

        # PWS-specific protein
        #c("GO_DEP_PWSTI", "GO_DEP_PWSTII"),

          # all Protein + RNA
        c("GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC", "GO_DEP_HetDel", "GO_DEP_HomoDel", "GO_DEP_PWSTI",
          "GO_DEP_PWSTII", "GO_DEG_DelC", "GO_DEG_DupC", "GO_DEG_HetDel",  "GO_DEG_HomoDel",
          "GO_DEG_PWSTI", "GO_DEG_PWSTII"),
        
        # all Ubi + Prot
        c("GO_DEU_DelC", "GO_DEU_DupC", "GO_DEU_HomoDel", "GO_DEU_PWSTI", "GO_DEU_PWSTII",
          "GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC", "GO_DEP_HetDel",  "GO_DEP_HomoDel", "GO_DEP_PWSTI", "GO_DEP_PWSTII"),

        # all RNA + Prot + Ubi
        c("GO_DEU_DelC", "GO_DEU_DupC", "GO_DEU_HomoDel", "GO_DEU_PWSTI", "GO_DEU_PWSTII",
          "GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC", "GO_DEP_HetDel",  "GO_DEP_HomoDel", "GO_DEP_PWSTI",
          "GO_DEP_PWSTII", "GO_DEG_DelC", "GO_DEG_DupC", "GO_DEG_HetDel", "GO_DEG_HomoDel",
          "GO_DEG_PWSTI", "GO_DEG_PWSTII")
    )

UpSetR::upset(
  fromList(GO_downregulated_RNA_prot_ubi),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  order.by = "degree",
  decreasing = FALSE,
  sets.bar.color = "black",
  matrix.color = "black",
  mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size",
  sets.x.label = "Set Size",
  intersections = intersectionsGO_RNA_Prot_Ubi  # Add this line
)


#Query intersections of interest

GO_downregulated_binary_RNA_prot_ubi <- fromList(GO_downregulated_RNA_prot_ubi)

GO_downregulated_binary_RNA_prot_ubi_with_intersection <-
  GO_downregulated_binary_RNA_prot_ubi_with_intersection %>%
```

7.11 Length of the lists - RNA

```{r}
# --- 1. Combine RNA GO Lists ---
# This creates a new list where each element is the union of up/down terms
# for that specific experimental condition.

GO_combined_onlyRNA <- list(
  GO_DEG_DelC = union(GO_upregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_DelC, GO_downregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_DelC),
  GO_DEG_DupC = union(GO_upregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_DupC, GO_downregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_DupC),
  GO_DEG_HetDel = union(GO_upregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_HetDel, GO_downregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_HetDel),
  GO_DEG_HomoDel = union(GO_upregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_HomoDel, GO_downregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_HomoDel),
  GO_DEG_PWSTI = union(GO_upregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_PWSTI, GO_downregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_PWSTI),
  GO_DEG_PWSTII = union(GO_upregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_PWSTII, GO_downregulated_onlyRNA$GO_DEG_PWSTII)
)

# --- 2. Get the length (count) of unique terms in each combined list ---
# sapply is used to apply the 'length' function to each element of the list

cat("--- Total Unique RNA GO Terms (Up + Down) ---\n")
print(sapply(GO_combined_onlyRNA, length))
```

7.12 Length of the lists - Protein

```{r}
# --- 1. Combine Protein GO Lists ---

GO_combined_onlyProtein <- list(
  GO_DEP_DelC = union(GO_upregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_DelC, GO_downregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_DelC),
  GO_DEP_DupC = union(GO_upregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_DupC, GO_downregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_DupC),
  GO_DEP_HetDel = union(GO_upregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_HetDel, GO_downregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_HetDel),
  GO_DEP_HomoDel = union(GO_upregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_HomoDel, GO_downregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_HomoDel),
  GO_DEP_PWSTI = union(GO_upregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_PWSTI, GO_downregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_PWSTI),
  GO_DEP_PWSTII = union(GO_upregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_PWSTII, GO_downregulated_onlyProtein$GO_DEP_PWSTII)
)

# --- 2. Get the length (count) of unique terms in each combined list ---

cat("--- Total Unique Protein GO Terms (Up + Down) ---\n")
print(sapply(GO_combined_onlyProtein, length))
```

```{r}
  GO_DEU_DelC = union(GO_upregulated_onlyUbi$GO_DEU_DelC, GO_downregulated_onlyUbi$GO_DEU_DelC),
```

```{r}
        # PWS-specific protein

          # all Protein + RNA
        c("GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC", "GO_DEP_HetDel", "GO_DEP_HomoDel", "GO_DEP_PWSTI",
          "GO_DEP_PWSTII", "GO_DEG_DelC", "GO_DEG_DupC", "GO_DEG_HetDel",  "GO_DEG_HomoDel",
          "GO_DEG_PWSTI", "GO_DEG_PWSTII"),
        
        # all Ubi + Prot
        c("GO_DEU_DelC", "GO_DEU_DupC", "GO_DEU_HomoDel", "GO_DEU_PWSTI", "GO_DEU_PWSTII",
          "GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC", "GO_DEP_HetDel",  "GO_DEP_HomoDel", "GO_DEP_PWSTI", "GO_DEP_PWSTII"),

        # all RNA + Prot + Ubi
        c("GO_DEU_DelC", "GO_DEU_DupC", "GO_DEU_HomoDel", "GO_DEU_PWSTI", "GO_DEU_PWSTII",
          "GO_DEP_DelC", "GO_DEP_DupC", "GO_DEP_HetDel",  "GO_DEP_HomoDel", "GO_DEP_PWSTI",

# Create the UpSet plot with specific intersections
UpSetR::upset(

GO_upregulated_binary_RNA_prot_ubi <- fromList(GO_upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi)
GO_upregulated_binary_RNA_prot_ubi$GO <- unique(unlist(GO_upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi))
```

8.1 Enrichment of SFARI genes in DEG intersection

```{r}


# --- Part A: Load and Prepare All Data ---

common_genes <- Reduce(intersect, list_RNA_DE_overlap)


# 2. Define the background gene set from your DESeq2 object
background_genes <- rownames(dds_RNA)

keep <- rowSums(counts(dds_RNA)) >= 10
dds_filtered <- dds_RNA[keep, ]

# Now, define the background using the rownames of this filtered object.
# This is your definitive, "reviewer-proof" background list.
background_genes_rigorous <- rownames(dds_filtered)


# --- Confirm the difference ---
message(paste("Original number of genes in dds_RNA:", nrow(dds_RNA)))
message(paste("Number of expressed genes after filtering (the rigorous background):", length(background_genes_rigorous)))

# 3. Load the SFARI ASD gene list
sfari_file_path <- "SFARI-Gene_genes_04-03-2025release_07-03-2025export.csv"
sfari_data <- read_csv(sfari_file_path)
asd_gene_list <- sfari_data$`gene-symbol`

# 4. Filter the SFARI list to only include genes present in your RNA-seq background
filtered_asd_gene_list_rna <- intersect(asd_gene_list, background_genes_rigorous)


# --- Part B: Local Enrichment Analysis Function ---
# This function performs a hypergeometric test locally. We can reuse it.

run_hypergeometric_test <- function(query_list, reference_list, background_list) {
  # q: The number of overlapping genes.
  q <- length(intersect(query_list, reference_list))
  
  # m: The total number of genes in the reference list (e.g., SFARI genes).
  m <- length(reference_list)
  
  # n: The number of genes in the background NOT in the reference list.
  n <- length(background_list) - m
  
  # k: The total number of genes in your query list.
  k <- length(query_list)
  
  # Perform the hypergeometric test for over-representation (enrichment).
  p_value <- phyper(q - 1, m, n, k, lower.tail = FALSE)
  
  # Get the list of overlapping genes
  overlapping_genes <- intersect(query_list, reference_list)
  
  # Return a list with the results
  return(list(
    p_value = p_value,
    overlapping_gene_count = q,
    query_list_size = k,
    reference_list_size = m,
    overlapping_genes = overlapping_genes
  ))
}


# --- Part C: Run the Analysis for the Combined RNA-Seq Data ---

print("--- Analyzing Combined UP- and DOWN-Regulated Genes (RNA-Seq) ---")
results_rna_combined <- run_hypergeometric_test(
  query_list = common_genes,
  reference_list = filtered_asd_gene_list_rna,
  background_list = background_genes_rigorous
)


# --- Part D: View the Final Results ---

print("---------------------------------------------------------")
print("Enrichment Analysis Results for Combined Genes (RNA-Seq):")
print(paste("P-value:", results_rna_combined$p_value))
print(paste("Number of overlapping genes:", results_rna_combined$overlapping_gene_count, "out of", results_rna_combined$query_list_size))
print("Overlapping Genes:")
print(results_rna_combined$overlapping_genes)
print("---------------------------------------------------------")
```

8.2 Enrichment of SFARI genes in DEG intersection - Heatmap of dysregulated genes

```{r}


# --- Step 1 & 2: Set up and Extract Data ---
mutant_genotypes <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")
lfc_col_pattern <- "genotype_%s_vs_WT"
pval_col_pattern <- "WaldPvalue_genotype_%s_vs_WT"

lfc_cols <- sapply(mutant_genotypes, function(m) sprintf(lfc_col_pattern, m))
pval_cols <- sapply(mutant_genotypes, function(m) sprintf(pval_col_pattern, m))

if (!all(lfc_cols %in% names(rowData(dds_RNA))) || !all(pval_cols %in% names(rowData(dds_RNA)))) {
  stop("One or more LFC/P-value columns not found in rowData(dds_RNA). Please check names.")
}

lfc_matrix <- as.data.frame(rowData(dds_RNA)[, lfc_cols])
pval_matrix <- as.data.frame(rowData(dds_RNA)[, pval_cols])
rownames(lfc_matrix) <- rownames(dds_RNA)
rownames(pval_matrix) <- rownames(dds_RNA)

padj_matrix <- as.data.frame(sapply(pval_matrix, function(pcol) p.adjust(pcol, method = "BH")))
rownames(padj_matrix) <- rownames(dds_RNA)
colnames(padj_matrix) <- paste0("padj_", mutant_genotypes, "_vs_WT")


final_gene_list_rna <- results_rna_combined$overlapping_genes

# A quick check to ensure the genes exist in the dataset
genes_in_data <- final_gene_list_rna %in% rownames(dds_RNA)
if (!all(genes_in_data)) {
  warning("Some enriched genes were not found in the dds_RNA object and will be excluded.")
  final_gene_list_rna <- final_gene_list_rna[genes_in_data]
}

if (length(final_gene_list_rna) > 0) {
  message(sprintf("Selected the %d SFARI-enriched genes for the heatmap.", length(final_gene_list_rna)))

  # --- Step 6: Get VST-transformed expression matrix ---
  message("Performing Variance Stabilizing Transformation...")
  vsd_rna <- vst(dds_RNA, blind = FALSE)
  vst_matrix_rna <- assay(vsd_rna)

  # --- Step 7: Subset VST matrix to selected genes ---
  heatmap_matrix_rna_unscaled <- vst_matrix_rna[final_gene_list_rna, , drop = FALSE]

  # --- Step 8: Calculate Z-scores PER BACKGROUND ---
  message("Calculating Z-scores separately for each background...")
  col_data <- as.data.frame(colData(dds_RNA))
  mgh_cols <- rownames(col_data[col_data$background == "MGH", ])
  b8330_cols <- rownames(col_data[col_data$background == "8330", ])

  mgh_matrix_unscaled <- heatmap_matrix_rna_unscaled[, mgh_cols, drop = FALSE]
  b8330_matrix_unscaled <- heatmap_matrix_rna_unscaled[, b8330_cols, drop = FALSE]

  calculate_z_score <- function(mat) {
    mat_scaled <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(mat), ncol = ncol(mat), dimnames = dimnames(mat))
    variances <- apply(mat, 1, var, na.rm=TRUE)
    has_variance <- variances > 1e-6
    if(any(has_variance)) {
      mat_subset <- mat[has_variance, , drop = FALSE]
      scaled_subset <- t(scale(t(mat_subset), center = TRUE, scale = TRUE))
      mat_scaled[has_variance, ] <- scaled_subset
    }
    return(mat_scaled)
  }

  z_mgh <- calculate_z_score(mgh_matrix_unscaled)
  z_8330 <- calculate_z_score(b8330_matrix_unscaled)

  z_score_matrix_combined <- cbind(z_mgh, z_8330)
  z_score_matrix_final <- z_score_matrix_combined[, colnames(heatmap_matrix_rna_unscaled)]

  # --- Step 9: Cap Z-scores to the range [-2, 2] ---
  message("Capping Z-scores to a range of -2 to 2.")
  z_score_matrix_final[z_score_matrix_final > 2] <- 2
  z_score_matrix_final[z_score_matrix_final < -2] <- -2

  # --- Step 10: Create Column Annotations and Colors ---
  col_annotation_rna <- as.data.frame(colData(dds_RNA)[, c("background", "genotype")])

  # Function to replicate ggplot's default color palette for consistency
  gg_color_hue <- function(n) {
    hues = seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
    hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100)[1:n]
  }

  genotype_levels <- levels(col_annotation_rna$genotype)
  genotype_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(genotype_levels)), genotype_levels)

  background_levels <- levels(col_annotation_rna$background)
  # Using a different palette for background to ensure clear distinction
  background_colors <- setNames(c("gray50", "gray80"), background_levels) 

  ann_colors_rna <- list(
    genotype = genotype_colors,
    background = background_colors
  )

  # --- NEW: Step 10.5: Define Custom Sample Order and Reorder Matrix ---
  message("Reordering matrix columns based on specified genotype order...")
  
  # 1. Define the desired order of genotypes
  desired_genotype_order <- c("WT", "DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")
  
  # 2. Re-level the genotype factor in the annotation to match your desired order
  col_annotation_rna$genotype <- factor(col_annotation_rna$genotype, levels = desired_genotype_order)
  
  # 3. Get the new column order by sorting the annotation frame
  #    This sorts by your custom genotype order first, then by background.
  ordered_sample_names <- rownames(col_annotation_rna[order(col_annotation_rna$genotype, col_annotation_rna$background), ])
  
  # 4. Reorder the main Z-score matrix according to this new sample order
  z_score_matrix_ordered <- z_score_matrix_final[, ordered_sample_names]

  # --- Step 11: Generate Heatmap  ---
  message("Generating heatmap with custom sample order (no column clustering)...")

  pheatmap_rna_ordered <- pheatmap(
    z_score_matrix_ordered,        
    annotation_col = col_annotation_rna,
    annotation_colors = ann_colors_rna,
    cluster_rows = TRUE,            
    cluster_cols = FALSE,          
    show_rownames = TRUE,
    show_colnames = TRUE,
    fontsize_row = 8,
    fontsize_col = 8,
    color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 10, name = "RdBu")))(100),
    main = "Heatmap of 29 SFARI-Enriched Genes (Custom Sample Order)",
    scale = "none",                 
    breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
    border_color = NA,
    legend_breaks = c(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)
  )

  print(pheatmap_rna_ordered)
  message("SFARI-enriched gene heatmap generation complete.")

} else {
  message("The list of enriched genes is empty. Cannot generate heatmap.")
}
```

8.3 Enrichment of SFARI genes for individual conditions

```{r}

# 1. Background gene set from your DESeq2 object
background_genes <- rownames(dds_RNA)

# 2. SFARI ASD gene list
sfari_file_path <- "SFARI-Gene_genes_04-03-2025release_07-03-2025export.csv"
sfari_data <- read_csv(sfari_file_path)
asd_gene_list <- sfari_data$`gene-symbol`

# 3. Filtered SFARI list (genes present in your background)
filtered_asd_gene_list_rna <- intersect(asd_gene_list, background_genes)


# --- Part B: Create a List of All Gene Sets to Test ---
# We'll put all the gene lists you want to analyze into this one main list.

gene_lists_to_test <- list(
  "Overlapping_DEG" = common_genes,
  "DEG_DelC"              = list_RNA_DE_overlap$overlap_DEG_DelC,
  "DEG_DupC"              = list_RNA_DE_overlap$overlap_DEG_DupC,
  "DEG_HetDel"            = list_RNA_DE_overlap$overlap_DEG_HetDel,
  "DEG_HomoDel"           = list_RNA_DE_overlap$overlap_DEG_HomoDel,
  "DEG_PWSTI"             = list_RNA_DE_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTI,
  "DEG_PWSTII"            = list_RNA_DE_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTII
)


# --- Part C: Local Enrichment Analysis Function (Unchanged) ---
# We will reuse the same robust function from before.

run_hypergeometric_test <- function(query_list, reference_list, background_list) {
  q <- length(intersect(query_list, reference_list))
  m <- length(reference_list)
  n <- length(background_list) - m
  k <- length(query_list)
  p_value <- phyper(q - 1, m, n, k, lower.tail = FALSE)
  overlapping_genes <- intersect(query_list, reference_list)
  return(list(p_value = p_value, overlap_count = q, query_size = k, genes = overlapping_genes))
}


# --- Part D: Loop Through and Run All Analyses ---
# We use lapply to run the test on every item in our 'gene_lists_to_test'.

all_results <- lapply(gene_lists_to_test, function(gene_list) {
  run_hypergeometric_test(
    query_list = gene_list,
    reference_list = filtered_asd_gene_list_rna,
    background_list = background_genes_rigorous
  )
})


# --- Part E: Display All Results in a Clear Format ---
# Loop through the results and print them with headers.

for (name in names(all_results)) {
  result <- all_results[[name]]
  
  cat("---------------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(paste("Enrichment Results for:", name, "\n"))
  cat("---------------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(paste("P-value:", formatC(result$p_value, format = "e", digits = 3), "\n"))
  cat(paste("Overlap:", result$overlap_count, "genes out of", result$query_size, "\n"))
  cat("Overlapping Genes:", paste(result$genes, collapse = ", "), "\n\n")
}
```

8.4 Enrichment of SFARI genes for individual conditions - Preparation for Plotting

```{r}
# Convert the list of results into a single data frame
results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(all_results), function(name) {
  data.frame(
    ListName = name,
    PValue = all_results[[name]]$p_value,
    OverlapCount = all_results[[name]]$overlap_count,
    QuerySize = all_results[[name]]$query_size,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

# Calculate additional metrics needed for the plots
background_size <- length(background_genes_rigorous)
reference_size <- length(filtered_asd_gene_list_rna)

results_df <- results_df %>%
  mutate(
    GeneRatio = OverlapCount / QuerySize,
    ExpectedOverlap = QuerySize * (reference_size / background_size),
    FoldEnrichment = OverlapCount / ExpectedOverlap,
    Log10PValue = -log10(PValue)
  ) %>%
  # Reorder the list names based on P-value for cleaner plots
  mutate(ListName = fct_reorder(ListName, PValue))

# View the final data frame
print("Summary data frame for plotting:")
print(results_df)
```

8.5 Lollipop Plot

```{r}
# Enrichment of SFARI genes for individual conditions - Lollipop Plot


# --- Generate the Lollipop Plot ---

# Reorder factor levels for plotting based on Fold Enrichment
results_df_lollipop <- results_df %>%
  mutate(ListName = fct_reorder(ListName, FoldEnrichment))

lollipop_plot <- ggplot(results_df_lollipop, aes(x = FoldEnrichment, y = ListName)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = FoldEnrichment, y = ListName, yend = ListName), color = "gray") +
  geom_point(aes(color = Log10PValue), size = 5) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "firebrick", name = "-log10(P-value)") +
  labs(
    title = "Fold Enrichment for ASD-Associated Genes",
    subtitle = "How many times more overlap than expected by chance",
    x = "Fold Enrichment",
    y = "Gene List"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"))

print(lollipop_plot)
```

8.6 Bar chart

```{r}
#12.6 Enrichment of SFARI genes for individual conditions - Bar chart


# --- Step 1: Reshape data for plotting ---
plot_data_bars <- results_df %>%
  dplyr::select(ListName, Observed = OverlapCount, Expected = ExpectedOverlap) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("Observed", "Expected"),
    names_to = "Category",
    values_to = "Count"
  )

# --- Step 2: Generate the Bar Chart ---
comparative_bar_chart <- ggplot(plot_data_bars, aes(x = ListName, y = Count, fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Observed vs. Expected Overlap with ASD Genes",
    x = "Gene List",
    y = "Number of Genes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Observed" = "firebrick", "Expected" = "gray")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"))

print(comparative_bar_chart)
```

8.7 SFARI v2

```{r}
# --- Step 1: Create Strict "Same-Direction" Overlaps ---

# 1. Intersect UP regulated lists (MGH Up AND 8330 Up)
list_RNA_UP_strict <- list(
  "DelC"    = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_up$RNA_DelC_up,       list_8330_RNA_up$RNA_DelC_up),
  "DupC"    = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_up$RNA_DupC_up,       list_8330_RNA_up$RNA_DupC_up),
  "HetDel"  = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_up$RNA_HetDel_up,     list_8330_RNA_up$RNA_HetDel_up),
  "HomoDel" = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_up$RNA_HomoDel_up,    list_8330_RNA_up$RNA_HomoDel_up),
  "PWSTI"   = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_up$RNA_PWSTI_up,      list_8330_RNA_up$RNA_PWSTI_up),
  "PWSTII"  = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_up$RNA_PWSTII_up,     list_8330_RNA_up$RNA_PWSTII_up)
)

# 2. Intersect DOWN regulated lists (MGH Down AND 8330 Down)
list_RNA_DOWN_strict <- list(
  "DelC"    = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_DelC_down,    list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_DelC_down),
  "DupC"    = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_DupC_down,    list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_DupC_down),
  "HetDel"  = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_HetDel_down,  list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_HetDel_down),
  "HomoDel" = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_HomoDel_down, list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_HomoDel_down),
  "PWSTI"   = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_PWSTI_down,   list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_PWSTI_down),
  "PWSTII"  = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_PWSTII_down,  list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_PWSTII_down)
)

# 3. Combine them into "Same-Direction" lists per condition
#    (Genes that are either consistently UP or consistently DOWN)
list_RNA_SameDir_overlap <- list()
conditions <- names(list_RNA_UP_strict)

for(cond in conditions){
  # Union of the strict Up and strict Down for this condition
  list_RNA_SameDir_overlap[[paste0("Strict_DEG_", cond)]] <- union(
    list_RNA_UP_strict[[cond]], 
    list_RNA_DOWN_strict[[cond]]
  )
}

# 4. Create the "Core Intersection" (Genes DE in same direction in ALL mutants)
#    Note: This might be a very small list!
all_strict_genes <- unlist(list_RNA_SameDir_overlap)
common_genes_strict <- Reduce(intersect, list_RNA_SameDir_overlap)

# Check sizes
print("Size of strict same-direction overlap lists:")
print(sapply(list_RNA_SameDir_overlap, length))


# --- Part A: Define Background & Load SFARI (Same as before) ---

# 1. Define Background
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds_RNA)) >= 10
dds_filtered <- dds_RNA[keep, ]
background_genes_rigorous <- rownames(dds_filtered)

# 2. Load SFARI
sfari_file_path <- "SFARI-Gene_genes_04-03-2025release_07-03-2025export.csv"
sfari_data <- read_csv(sfari_file_path)
asd_gene_list <- sfari_data$`gene-symbol`

# 3. Filter SFARI
filtered_asd_gene_list_rna <- intersect(asd_gene_list, background_genes_rigorous)

# --- Part B: Hypergeometric Function (Reused) ---
run_hypergeometric_test <- function(query_list, reference_list, background_list) {
  q <- length(intersect(query_list, reference_list))
  m <- length(reference_list)
  n <- length(background_list) - m
  k <- length(query_list)
  p_value <- phyper(q - 1, m, n, k, lower.tail = FALSE)
  overlapping_genes <- intersect(query_list, reference_list)
  return(list(p_value = p_value, overlap_count = q, query_size = k, genes = overlapping_genes))
}

# --- Part C: Run Analysis on Individual Strict Lists ---

# Create list of gene sets to test
gene_lists_to_test_strict <- list(
  "Strict_DelC"    = list_RNA_SameDir_overlap$Strict_DEG_DelC,
  "Strict_DupC"    = list_RNA_SameDir_overlap$Strict_DEG_DupC,
  "Strict_HetDel"  = list_RNA_SameDir_overlap$Strict_DEG_HetDel,
  "Strict_HomoDel" = list_RNA_SameDir_overlap$Strict_DEG_HomoDel,
  "Strict_PWSTI"   = list_RNA_SameDir_overlap$Strict_DEG_PWSTI,
  "Strict_PWSTII"  = list_RNA_SameDir_overlap$Strict_DEG_PWSTII,
  "Strict_CORE"    = common_genes_strict # The intersection of all above
)

# Run tests
all_results_strict <- lapply(gene_lists_to_test_strict, function(gene_list) {
  # Skip empty lists to avoid errors
  if(length(gene_list) == 0) return(list(p_value=1, overlap_count=0, query_size=0, genes=character(0)))
  
  run_hypergeometric_test(
    query_list = gene_list, 
    reference_list = filtered_asd_gene_list_rna, 
    background_list = background_genes_rigorous
  )
})

# Print Results
for (name in names(all_results_strict)) {
  result <- all_results_strict[[name]]
  cat("---------------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(paste("Strict Enrichment Results for:", name, "\n"))
  cat(paste("P-value:", formatC(result$p_value, format = "e", digits = 3), "\n"))
  cat(paste("Overlap:", result$overlap_count, "genes out of", result$query_size, "\n"))
}


# --- Prepare Data for Plotting ---
results_df_strict <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(all_results_strict), function(name) {
  data.frame(
    ListName = name,
    PValue = all_results_strict[[name]]$p_value,
    OverlapCount = all_results_strict[[name]]$overlap_count,
    QuerySize = all_results_strict[[name]]$query_size,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

background_size <- length(background_genes_rigorous)
reference_size <- length(filtered_asd_gene_list_rna)

results_df_strict <- results_df_strict %>%
  mutate(
    GeneRatio = OverlapCount / QuerySize,
    ExpectedOverlap = QuerySize * (reference_size / background_size),
    FoldEnrichment = ifelse(ExpectedOverlap == 0, 0, OverlapCount / ExpectedOverlap),
    Log10PValue = -log10(PValue)
  ) %>%
  mutate(ListName = fct_reorder(ListName, FoldEnrichment)) # Order by Enrichment

# --- 1. Lollipop Plot (Strict) ---
lollipop_plot_strict <- ggplot(results_df_strict, aes(x = FoldEnrichment, y = ListName)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = FoldEnrichment, y = ListName, yend = ListName), color = "gray") +
  geom_point(aes(color = Log10PValue), size = 5) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "firebrick", name = "-log10(P)") +
  labs(
    title = "SFARI Enrichment (Strict Directional Overlap)",
    subtitle = "Genes must be Up/Up or Down/Down in both backgrounds",
    x = "Fold Enrichment",
    y = "Condition"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"))

print(lollipop_plot_strict)

# --- 2. Bar Chart (Observed vs Expected) ---
plot_data_bars_strict <- results_df_strict %>%
  dplyr::select(ListName, Observed = OverlapCount, Expected = ExpectedOverlap) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Observed", "Expected"), names_to = "Category", values_to = "Count")

bar_chart_strict <- ggplot(plot_data_bars_strict, aes(x = ListName, y = Count, fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Observed" = "#D55E00", "Expected" = "gray")) +
  labs(
    title = "Observed vs. Expected SFARI Genes (Strict Overlap)",
    y = "Number of Genes",
    x = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(bar_chart_strict)


# Extract the strict core SFARI genes
if (!is.null(all_results_strict$Strict_CORE)) {
  final_gene_list_rna_strict <- all_results_strict$Strict_CORE$genes
} else {
  final_gene_list_rna_strict <- character(0)
}

if (length(final_gene_list_rna_strict) > 0) {
  
  message(sprintf("Found %d strict core SFARI genes.", length(final_gene_list_rna_strict)))

  # --- VST and Z-score Calculation ---
  vsd_rna <- vst(dds_RNA, blind = FALSE)
  heatmap_matrix <- assay(vsd_rna)[final_gene_list_rna_strict, , drop = FALSE]

  # Calculate Z-scores per background to normalize batch effects
  col_data <- as.data.frame(colData(dds_RNA))
  
  # --- FIX: Renamed variable from '8330_cols' to 'b8330_cols' ---
  mgh_cols <- rownames(col_data[col_data$background == "MGH", ])
  b8330_cols <- rownames(col_data[col_data$background == "8330", ])
  
  # Function to calc Z-score
  calc_z <- function(mat) {
    if(nrow(mat) == 0) return(mat)
    # Apply scaling: transpose -> scale -> transpose back
    t(scale(t(mat)))
  }
  
  # Calculate Z-scores using the fixed variable name
  z_mgh <- calc_z(heatmap_matrix[, mgh_cols, drop=FALSE])
  z_8330 <- calc_z(heatmap_matrix[, b8330_cols, drop=FALSE])
  
  # Combine columns back together
  z_score_final <- cbind(z_mgh, z_8330)
  
  # Cap Z-scores at +/- 2 for better visualization contrast
  z_score_final[z_score_final > 2] <- 2
  z_score_final[z_score_final < -2] <- -2
  
  # Annotations
  ann_col <- col_data[, c("background", "genotype")]
  
  # Ensure custom order (WT first, then Mutants)
  desired_order <- c("WT", "DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")
  # Filter levels to only those actually present in the data to avoid errors
  existing_levels <- intersect(desired_order, levels(factor(ann_col$genotype)))
  ann_col$genotype <- factor(ann_col$genotype, levels = existing_levels)
  
  # Order columns by genotype then background
  ord <- rownames(ann_col[order(ann_col$genotype, ann_col$background), ])
  # Filter 'ord' to ensure we only use samples that are actually in our matrix
  ord <- intersect(ord, colnames(z_score_final))
  z_score_final <- z_score_final[, ord, drop=FALSE]

  # Plot
  pheatmap(
    z_score_final,
    annotation_col = ann_col,
    cluster_cols = FALSE,
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    show_rownames = TRUE,
    show_colnames = FALSE,
    color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(10, "RdBu")))(100),
    breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
    main = "Strict Core SFARI Genes (Consistent Direction)",
    border_color = NA
  )
  
} else {
  message("No genes found that are SFARI hits AND consistently DE in all mutants.")
}
```

8.8 Additional disease gene lists

```{r}
# --- Step 1: Load and Clean the Excel Data ---

# Read the Excel file
raw_excel_data <- read_excel("genelist_for_enrichment.xlsx", sheet = 1, .name_repair = "minimal")

# Convert columns to a clean list of vectors (Removing NAs and blanks)
reference_gene_lists <- lapply(raw_excel_data, function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)            # Ensure text format
  x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]     # Remove NAs and blanks
  return(unique(x))               # Ensure unique genes per list
})

print("Size of Reference Lists:")
print(sapply(reference_gene_lists, length))


# --- Step 2: Run Enrichment Analysis on New Lists ---

# Initialize a container for results
final_enrichment_table <- data.frame()

# Loop through each Reference List (SPARK, NDD, etc.)
for (ref_name in names(reference_gene_lists)) {
  
  # Filter reference list to background
  raw_ref_list <- reference_gene_lists[[ref_name]]
  filtered_ref_list <- intersect(raw_ref_list, background_genes_rigorous)
  
  # Skip if list is empty
  if(length(filtered_ref_list) == 0) {
    message(paste("Skipping", ref_name, "- No overlap with background."))
    next
  }
  
  # Test against ALL Strict Mutant Conditions
  list_results <- lapply(names(gene_lists_to_test_strict), function(strict_name) {
    
    query_genes <- gene_lists_to_test_strict[[strict_name]]
    if(length(query_genes) == 0) return(NULL) 
    
    # Run Hypergeometric Function (Assumes function exists from previous steps)
    res <- run_hypergeometric_test(
      query_list = query_genes,
      reference_list = filtered_ref_list,
      background_list = background_genes_rigorous
    )
    
    data.frame(
      ReferenceList = ref_name,
      Condition = strict_name,
      PValue = res$p_value,
      OverlapCount = res$overlap_count,
      QuerySize = res$query_size,
      RefSize = length(filtered_ref_list),
      BackgroundSize = length(background_genes_rigorous),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
  
  final_enrichment_table <- rbind(final_enrichment_table, do.call(rbind, list_results))
}

# Calculate Metrics for the new lists
final_enrichment_table <- final_enrichment_table %>%
  mutate(
    ExpectedOverlap = QuerySize * (RefSize / BackgroundSize),
    FoldEnrichment = ifelse(ExpectedOverlap == 0, 0, OverlapCount / ExpectedOverlap),
    Log10PValue = -log10(PValue)
  )


# --- Step 3: Combine with SFARI and Prepare for Plotting ---

# 1. Clean SFARI results (Renaming columns to match)
sfari_clean <- results_df_strict %>%
  mutate(ReferenceList = "SFARI") %>%
  dplyr::rename(Condition = ListName) %>%
  dplyr::select(ReferenceList, Condition, PValue, OverlapCount, 
                QuerySize, FoldEnrichment, Log10PValue)

# 2. Clean Multi-List results
multi_list_clean <- final_enrichment_table %>%
  dplyr::select(ReferenceList, Condition, PValue, OverlapCount, 
                QuerySize, FoldEnrichment, Log10PValue)

# 3. Combine them
all_enrichment_data <- rbind(sfari_clean, multi_list_clean)


# --- Step 4: Apply Filters and Fix Ordering ---

# A. Filter: Keep only significant results (P <= 0.05)
plot_data <- all_enrichment_data %>%
  filter(PValue <= 0.05) %>%
  # Cap P-value for cleaner plotting (anything > 10 is treated as 10)
  mutate(Log10PValue_Capped = ifelse(Log10PValue > 10, 10, Log10PValue))

# B. Fix Condition Order (Now on X-Axis)
correct_condition_order <- c("Strict_DelC", "Strict_DupC", "Strict_HetDel", 
                             "Strict_HomoDel", "Strict_PWSTI", "Strict_PWSTII", "Strict_CORE")

existing_levels <- intersect(correct_condition_order, unique(plot_data$Condition))
if(length(existing_levels) > 0) {
  plot_data$Condition <- factor(plot_data$Condition, levels = existing_levels)
}

# C. Fix ReferenceList Order (Now on Y-Axis)
# We put SFARI at the top or bottom depending on preference. 
# Here we ensure it's first in the factor levels.
ref_levels <- unique(plot_data$ReferenceList)
if("SFARI" %in% ref_levels) {
  ref_levels <- c("SFARI", setdiff(ref_levels, "SFARI"))
}
plot_data$ReferenceList <- factor(plot_data$ReferenceList, levels = ref_levels)


# --- Step 5: Generate the Heatmap (Swapped Axes) ---

heatmap_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Condition, y = ReferenceList)) + 
  # The Dot: Size = Effect Size, Color = Significance
  geom_point(aes(size = FoldEnrichment, color = Log10PValue_Capped)) +
  
  # Colors: Gray to Firebrick
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#E0E0E0", high = "firebrick", 
                       name = "-log10(P)",
                       limits = c(1.3, 10), 
                       oob = scales::squish) +
  
  # Size: Scale the dots
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Fold Enrichment", range = c(3, 12)) +
  
  # Aesthetics
  labs(
    title = "Strict Signal Validation",
    subtitle = "Comparing MGH/8330 Consistent Genes against Clinical Lists (P < 0.05)",
    x = "", 
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    # X-axis (Conditions) needs angle to fit
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    # Y-axis (Reference Lists) is now horizontal and easier to read
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linetype = "dotted"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.box = "vertical"
  )

print(heatmap_plot)
```

```{r}
# --- 1. Create Overlaps for UP-regulated sets ---

list_RNA_up_overlap <-

                                    list_8330_Protein_up$Protein_PWSTI_up),
    "overlap_DEP_PWSTII" = intersect(list_MGH_Protein_up$Protein_PWSTII_up,
                                     list_8330_Protein_up$Protein_PWSTII_up)

list_Ubi_up_overlap <-
  list(
    "overlap_DEU_DelC" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_DelC_up,
                                   list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_DelC_up),
    "overlap_DEU_DupC" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_DupC_up,
                                   list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_DupC_up),
    "overlap_DEU_HetDel" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_HetDel_up,
                                     list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_HetDel_up),
    "overlap_DEU_HomoDel" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_HomoDel_up,
                                      list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_HomoDel_up),
    "overlap_DEU_PWSTI" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_PWSTI_up,
                                    list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_PWSTI_up),
    "overlap_DEU_PWSTII" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_PWSTII_up,
                                     list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_PWSTII_up)
  )


# --- 2. Combine into Master List ---

upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi <- list(
  # DEG (Differentially Expressed Genes)
  DEG_DelC = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_DelC,
  DEG_DupC = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_DupC,
  DEG_HetDel = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_HetDel,
  DEG_HomoDel = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_HomoDel,
  DEG_PWSTI = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTI,
  DEG_PWSTII = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTII,
  
  # DEP (Differentially Expressed Proteins)
  DEP_DelC = list_Protein_up_overlap$overlap_DEP_DelC,
  DEP_DupC = list_Protein_up_overlap$overlap_DEP_DupC,
  DEP_HetDel = list_Protein_up_overlap$overlap_DEP_HetDel,
  DEP_HomoDel = list_Protein_up_overlap$overlap_DEP_HomoDel,
  DEP_PWSTI = list_Protein_up_overlap$overlap_DEP_PWSTI,
  DEP_PWSTII = list_Protein_up_overlap$overlap_DEP_PWSTII,
  
  # DEU (Differentially Expressed Ubiquitinated Proteins)
  # Note: HetDel is excluded here matching your original Down code structure
  DEU_DelC = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_DelC,
  DEU_DupC = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_DupC,
  DEU_HomoDel = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_HomoDel,
  DEU_PWSTI = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_PWSTI,
  DEU_PWSTII = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_PWSTII
)

# Create the First UpSet plot (Frequency order)
UpSetR::upset(
  fromList(upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi), 
  nsets = length(upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi),
  keep.order = TRUE, 
  order.by = "freq",
  sets.bar.color = "black",
  matrix.color = "black",
  mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size (UP)",
  sets.x.label = "Set Size"
)


# --- 3. Define Intersections for Second Plot ---
# Note: The names (DEG_DelC, etc.) remain the same as they are keys in the list,
# but the data inside them is now UP-regulated.

intersections_RNA_Prot_Ubi_UP <-
    list(
        # all RNA
        c("DEG_DelC", "DEG_DupC", "DEG_HetDel", "DEG_HomoDel", "DEG_PWSTI", "DEG_PWSTII"),

        # all Prot
        c("DEP_DelC", "DEP_DupC", "DEP_HetDel", "DEP_HomoDel", "DEP_PWSTI", "DEP_PWSTII"),

        # all Ubi
        c("DEU_DelC", "DEU_DupC", "DEU_HomoDel", "DEU_PWSTI", "DEU_PWSTII"),

        # all Protein + RNA
        c("DEP_DelC", "DEP_DupC", "DEP_HetDel", "DEP_HomoDel", "DEP_PWSTI", "DEP_PWSTII", 
          "DEG_DelC", "DEG_DupC", "DEG_HetDel", "DEG_HomoDel", "DEG_PWSTI", "DEG_PWSTII"),
        
        # all Ubi + Prot
        c("DEU_DelC", "DEU_DupC", "DEU_HomoDel", "DEU_PWSTI", "DEU_PWSTII",
          "DEP_DelC", "DEP_DupC", "DEP_HetDel", "DEP_HomoDel", "DEP_PWSTI", "DEP_PWSTII"),

        # all RNA + Prot + Ubi
        c("DEU_DelC", "DEU_DupC", "DEU_HomoDel", "DEU_PWSTI", "DEU_PWSTII",
          "DEP_DelC", "DEP_DupC", "DEP_HetDel", "DEP_HomoDel", "DEP_PWSTI", "DEP_PWSTII", 
          "DEG_DelC", "DEG_DupC", "DEG_HetDel", "DEG_HomoDel", "DEG_PWSTI", "DEG_PWSTII")
    )

# Create the Second UpSet plot (Degree order + Specific Intersections)
UpSetR::upset(
  fromList(upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  order.by = "degree",
  decreasing = FALSE,
  sets.bar.color = "black",
  matrix.color = "black",
  mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size (UP)",
  sets.x.label = "Set Size",
  intersections = intersections_RNA_Prot_Ubi_UP
)


# --- 4. Query Intersections of Interest ---

upregulated_complex_RNA_prot_ubi <- fromList(upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi) == 1
upregulated_complex_RNA_prot_ubi <- as.data.frame(upregulated_complex_RNA_prot_ubi)

  
upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df <- fromList(upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi)
upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df$gene <- unique(unlist(upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi))

upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection <- upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df %>%
  tidyr::unite(col = "intersection", -c("gene"), sep = "")

upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection <-
  upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection %>%
  group_by(intersection) %>%
  summarise(list = list(gene)) %>%
  mutate(list = setNames(list, intersection)) %>%
  pull(list)


# --- Retrieve Specific Sets ---
# The binary keys remain valid because the list structure (6 RNA, 6 Prot, 5 Ubi) is preserved.

# all (RNA + Prot + Ubi)
# (Length 17: 6+6+5)
upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection[["11111111111111111"]]

# all RNA
upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection[["11111100000000000"]]

# all Protein
upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection[["00000011111100000"]]

# all Ubi (Note: last 5 digits)
upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection[["00000000000011111"]]

# all RNA + Protein
upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection[["11111111111100000"]] 

#all RNA + Ubi
downregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection[["11111100000011111"]]

# all Ubi + Protein
downregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection[["00000011111111111"]]
```

```{r}
# --- Define Intersections (Standard for all plots) ---
# This ensures the bottom bars of the plot are always ordered meaningfully
intersections_RNA_Prot_Ubi_Mixed <- list(
  # all RNA
  c("DEG_DelC", "DEG_DupC", "DEG_HetDel", "DEG_HomoDel", "DEG_PWSTI", "DEG_PWSTII"),
  # all Prot
  c("DEP_DelC", "DEP_DupC", "DEP_HetDel", "DEP_HomoDel", "DEP_PWSTI", "DEP_PWSTII"),
  # all Ubi
  c("DEU_DelC", "DEU_DupC", "DEU_HomoDel", "DEU_PWSTI", "DEU_PWSTII"),
  # all Protein + RNA
  c("DEP_DelC", "DEP_DupC", "DEP_HetDel", "DEP_HomoDel", "DEP_PWSTI", "DEP_PWSTII", 
    "DEG_DelC", "DEG_DupC", "DEG_HetDel", "DEG_HomoDel", "DEG_PWSTI", "DEG_PWSTII"),
  # all Ubi + Prot
  c("DEU_DelC", "DEU_DupC", "DEU_HomoDel", "DEU_PWSTI", "DEU_PWSTII",
    "DEP_DelC", "DEP_DupC", "DEP_HetDel", "DEP_HomoDel", "DEP_PWSTI", "DEP_PWSTII"),
  # all RNA + Prot + Ubi
  c("DEU_DelC", "DEU_DupC", "DEU_HomoDel", "DEU_PWSTI", "DEU_PWSTII",
    "DEP_DelC", "DEP_DupC", "DEP_HetDel", "DEP_HomoDel", "DEP_PWSTI", "DEP_PWSTII", 
    "DEG_DelC", "DEG_DupC", "DEG_HetDel", "DEG_HomoDel", "DEG_PWSTI", "DEG_PWSTII")
)
```

```{r}
  # DEG (Down)
  DEG_DelC = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_DelC,
  DEG_DupC = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_DupC,
  DEG_HetDel = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_HetDel,
  DEG_HomoDel = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_HomoDel,
  DEG_PWSTI = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTI,
  DEG_PWSTII = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTII,
  
  # DEP (Down)
  DEP_DelC = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_DelC,
  DEP_DupC = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_DupC,
  DEP_HetDel = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_HetDel,
  DEP_HomoDel = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_HomoDel,
  DEP_PWSTI = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_PWSTI,
  DEP_PWSTII = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_PWSTII,
  
  # DEU (Up)
  DEU_DelC = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_DelC,
  DEU_DupC = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_DupC,
  DEU_HomoDel = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_HomoDel,
  DEU_PWSTI = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_PWSTI,
  DEU_PWSTII = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_PWSTII
)

  fromList(list_TD_PD_UU), nsets = length(list_TD_PD_UU), keep.order = TRUE, order.by = "freq",
  sets.bar.color = "black", matrix.color = "black", mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size (TD/PD/UU)", sets.x.label = "Set Size"
)

  intersections = intersections_RNA_Prot_Ubi_Mixed
```
