```{r}
# --- Query Intersections ---
df_TU_PU_UD <- fromList(list_TU_PU_UD)
df_TU_PU_UD$gene <- unique(unlist(list_TU_PU_UD))

df_TU_PU_UD_isect <- df_TU_PU_UD %>%
  tidyr::unite(col = "intersection", -c("gene"), sep = "") %>%
  group_by(intersection) %>%
  summarise(list = list(gene)) %>%
  mutate(list = setNames(list, intersection)) %>%
  pull(list)

# 1. All 3
df_TU_PU_UD_isect[["11111111111111111"]]
# 2. All RNA
df_TU_PU_UD_isect[["11111100000000000"]]
# 3. All Protein
df_TU_PU_UD_isect[["00000011111100000"]]
# 4. All Ubi
df_TU_PU_UD_isect[["00000000000011111"]]
# 5. All RNA + Protein
df_TU_PU_UD_isect[["11111111111100000"]]
# 6. All RNA + Ubi
df_TU_PU_UD_isect[["11111100000011111"]]
# 7. All Protein + Ubi
df_TU_PU_UD_isect[["00000011111111111"]]
```

9.9 RNA UP / Prot Down / Ubi UP

```{r}
# --- 6. Create List: RNA UP / Prot Down / Ubi UP ---
list_TU_PD_UU <- list(
  # DEG (UP)
  DEG_DelC = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_DelC,
  DEG_DupC = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_DupC,
  DEG_HetDel = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_HetDel,
  DEG_HomoDel = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_HomoDel,
  DEG_PWSTI = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTI,
  DEG_PWSTII = list_RNA_up_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTII,
  
  # DEP (Down)
  DEP_DelC = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_DelC,
  DEP_DupC = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_DupC,
  DEP_HetDel = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_HetDel,
  DEP_HomoDel = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_HomoDel,
  DEP_PWSTI = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_PWSTI,
  DEP_PWSTII = list_Protein_down_overlap$overlap_DEP_PWSTII,
  
  # DEU (UP)
  DEU_DelC = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_DelC,
  DEU_DupC = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_DupC,
  DEU_HomoDel = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_HomoDel,
  DEU_PWSTI = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_PWSTI,
  DEU_PWSTII = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_PWSTII
)

# Plot 1: Frequency
UpSetR::upset(
  fromList(list_TU_PD_UU), nsets = length(list_TU_PD_UU), keep.order = TRUE, order.by = "freq",
  sets.bar.color = "black", matrix.color = "black", mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size (TU/PD/UU)", sets.x.label = "Set Size"
)

# Plot 2: Intersections
UpSetR::upset(
  fromList(list_TU_PD_UU), keep.order = TRUE, order.by = "degree", decreasing = FALSE,
  sets.bar.color = "black", matrix.color = "black", mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size (TU/PD/UU)", sets.x.label = "Set Size",
  intersections = intersections_RNA_Prot_Ubi_Mixed
)

# --- Query Intersections ---
df_TU_PD_UU <- fromList(list_TU_PD_UU)
df_TU_PD_UU$gene <- unique(unlist(list_TU_PD_UU))

df_TU_PD_UU_isect <- df_TU_PD_UU %>%
  tidyr::unite(col = "intersection", -c("gene"), sep = "") %>%
  group_by(intersection) %>%
  summarise(list = list(gene)) %>%
  mutate(list = setNames(list, intersection)) %>%
  pull(list)

# 1. All 3
df_TU_PD_UU_isect[["11111111111111111"]]
# 2. All RNA
df_TU_PD_UU_isect[["11111100000000000"]]
# 3. All Protein
df_TU_PD_UU_isect[["00000011111100000"]]
# 4. All Ubi
df_TU_PD_UU_isect[["00000000000011111"]]
# 5. All RNA + Protein
df_TU_PD_UU_isect[["11111111111100000"]]
# 6. All RNA + Ubi
df_TU_PD_UU_isect[["11111100000011111"]]
# 7. All Protein + Ubi
df_TU_PD_UU_isect[["00000011111111111"]]
```

10.1 Number of deleted genes in PWST TI

```{r}

# 2. Connect to the Ensembl Database (Human - GRCh38/hg38)
ensembl <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")

# 3. Define the PWS Type 1 Deletion Coordinates
# These match the Mim et al. (2024) paper for BP1-BP3
chrom <- "15"
start_bp <- 23123714
end_bp   <- 28193120

# 4. Query the Database
# We ask for: Gene Name, Biotype (protein vs RNA), and Position
pws_genes <- getBM(
  attributes = c('hgnc_symbol', 'ensembl_gene_id', 'gene_biotype', 
                 'start_position', 'end_position'),
  filters = c('chromosome_name', 'start', 'end'),
  values = list(chrom, start_bp, end_bp),
  mart = ensembl
)

# 5. Clean Data: Filter to ensure genes are strictly within the region
pws_genes_strict <- subset(pws_genes, 
                           start_position >= start_bp & 
                           end_position <= end_bp)

# RESULTS

# A. TOTAL GENE COUNT
cat("Total genes found in PWS Type 1 Locus:", nrow(pws_genes_strict), "\n\n")

# B. COUNT BY TYPE (The Breakdown)
counts <- table(pws_genes_strict$gene_biotype)
print(as.data.frame(counts))

# Search for the SNORD116 cluster to see the copy numbers
snord116_variants <- pws_genes_strict[grep("SNORD116", pws_genes_strict$hgnc_symbol), ]

cat("\n--- SNORD116 VARIANTS FOUND ---\n")
print(snord116_variants[, c("hgnc_symbol", "gene_biotype")])

# Check if  specific variant 'SNORD116-7' exists in the list
has_variant_7 <- "SNORD116-7" %in% snord116_variants$hgnc_symbol
cat("\nIs SNORD116-7 present in this list? ", has_variant_7, "\n")
```

10.2 Upset Plots - Genes - RNA only - Preparation

```{r}
intersectionsOI_onlyRNA <-
  list(
    #single
    "DEG_DelC", 
    "DEG_DupC", 
    "DEG_HetDel",
    "DEG_HomoDel", 
    "DEG_PWSTI", 
    "DEG_PWSTII",

    #SYS-specific RNA
    c("DEG_DelC", "DEG_DupC"),

    #MAGEL2-del specific RNA
    c("DEG_HetDel", "DEG_HomoDel"),

    #PWS-specific RNA
    c("DEG_PWSTI", "DEG_PWSTII"),

    #deletion-specific RNA
    c("DEG_HetDel", "DEG_HomoDel",
         "DEG_PWSTI", "DEG_PWSTII"),

    #all RNA without DelC
    c("DEG_DupC",
         "DEG_HetDel","DEG_HomoDel",
         "DEG_PWSTI","DEG_PWSTII"),

    #all RNA
    c("DEG_DelC","DEG_DupC",
         "DEG_HetDel","DEG_HomoDel",
         "DEG_PWSTI","DEG_PWSTII")

    )
```

10.3 Overlap analysis: Upset Plots DEG - Downregulated

```{r}
list_RNA_down_overlap <-
  list(
    "overlap_DEG_DelC" = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_DelC_down,
                                   list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_DelC_down),
    "overlap_DEG_DupC" = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_DupC_down,
                                   list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_DupC_down),
    "overlap_DEG_HetDel" = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_HetDel_down,
                                   list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_HetDel_down),
    "overlap_DEG_HomoDel" = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_HomoDel_down,
                                   list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_HomoDel_down),
    "overlap_DEG_PWSTI" = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_PWSTI_down,
                                   list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_PWSTI_down),
    "overlap_DEG_PWSTII" = intersect(list_MGH_RNA_down$RNA_PWSTII_down,
                                   list_8330_RNA_down$RNA_PWSTII_down)
  )


downregulated_RNAonly <- list(
  DEG_DelC = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_DelC,
  DEG_DupC = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_DupC,
  DEG_HetDel = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_HetDel,
  DEG_HomoDel = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_HomoDel,
  DEG_PWSTI = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTI,
  DEG_PWSTII = list_RNA_down_overlap$overlap_DEG_PWSTII
  )


UpSetR::upset(
  fromList(downregulated_RNAonly),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  order.by = "degree",
  decreasing = FALSE,
  sets.bar.color = "black",
  matrix.color = "black",
  mainbar.y.label = "Intersection Size",
  sets.x.label = "Set Size",
  intersections = intersectionsOI_onlyRNA
)


downregulated_complex_RNAonly <- fromList(downregulated_RNAonly) == 1
downregulated_complex_RNAonly <- as.data.frame(downregulated_complex_RNAonly)

upset(downregulated_complex_RNAonly,
      colnames(downregulated_complex_RNAonly),
      sort_sets = FALSE,
      intersections = intersectionsOI_onlyRNA,
      sort_intersections=FALSE,
      theme = my_corrected_theme,
      queries=list(
        upset_query(intersect=intersectionsOI_onlyRNA[[2]], color="#EDF3FF"),
        upset_query(intersect=intersectionsOI_onlyRNA[[9]], color="#CADDFF"),
        upset_query(intersect=intersectionsOI_onlyRNA[[11]], color="#AAC7FF"),
        upset_query(intersect=intersectionsOI_onlyRNA[[12]], color="#8BAEFF")
        ))
  
downregulated_RNAonly_df <- fromList(downregulated_RNAonly)
downregulated_RNAonly_df$gene <- unique(unlist(downregulated_RNAonly))

downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection <- downregulated_RNAonly_df %>%
  tidyr::unite(col = "intersection", -c("gene"), sep = "")

downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection <-
  downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection %>%
  group_by(intersection) %>%
  summarise(list = list(gene)) %>%
  mutate(list = setNames(list, intersection)) %>%
  pull(list)

#DelC-specific
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["100000"]]

#DupC-specific
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["010000"]]

#SYS-specific RNA

#HetDel-specific
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["001000"]]

#HomoDel-specific
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["000100"]]

#MAGEL2-del-specific RNA
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["001100"]]

#PWSTI-specific
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["000010"]]

#PWSTII-specific
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["000001"]]

#PWS only
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["000011"]]

#PWS and MAGEL2-del gene only
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["011111"]]

downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["011111"]]

#all RNA
downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["111111"]]
```

```{r}
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["100000"]]

# DupC-specific
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["010000"]]

# SYS-specific Protein (DelC + DupC)
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["110000"]]

# HetDel-specific
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["001000"]]

# HomoDel-specific
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["000100"]]

# MAGEL2-del-specific Protein (HetDel + HomoDel)
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["001100"]]

# PWSTI-specific
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["000010"]]

# PWSTII-specific
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["000001"]]

# PWS only (PWSTI + PWSTII)
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["000011"]]

# PWS and MAGEL2-del gene only (Indices 3,4,5,6)
# Binary: 001111 (Note: 011111 includes DupC)
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["001111"]]

# All Except DelC (Indices 2,3,4,5,6)
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["011111"]]

# All Protein (Intersection of all 6)
upregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection[["111111"]]
```

11.1 GO analysis for PWS-specific genes excluding the genes that are in the locus

```{r}
txdb <- TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene

# Define the genomic range of interest
query_range <- GRanges(seqnames = "chr15", ranges = IRanges(start = 22480000, end = 28500000))

# Find genes within the specified genomic range
genes_in_region <- subsetByOverlaps(transcripts(txdb), query_range)


# Connect to Ensembl
ensembl <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")

# Extract the ENST IDs from your GRanges object and remove the version numbers
enst_ids <- as.character(mcols(genes_in_region)$tx_name)
enst_ids <- sub("\\..*", "", enst_ids)

# Retrieve the gene names based on the ENST IDs
gen_info <- getBM(attributes = c("ensembl_transcript_id", "hgnc_symbol"),
                  filters = "ensembl_transcript_id",
                  values = enst_ids,
                  mart = ensembl)

# Convert the result into a user-friendly list
PWSgene_names_list <- setNames(as.list(gen_info$hgnc_symbol), gen_info$ensembl_transcript_id)

# Output the gene names
PWSgene_names_list

# Remove the ENST IDs, so that only the gene names remain
PWSgene_names_only <- unname(PWSgene_names_list)

# Output the gene names
PWSgene_names_only

# Remove the ENST IDs and keep only unique gene names
PWSgene_names_unique <- unique(unname(PWSgene_names_list))

# Output the unique gene names
PWSgene_names_unique

# Remove the PWS locus genes from the PWS-specific DEG list and create the new list
minusPWS_downregulated_df_with_intersection <- setdiff(downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["000011"]], PWSgene_names_unique); length(minusPWS_downregulated_df_with_intersection)

# Specify the genes to remove
genes_to_remove <- c("PWRN3", "PWARSN", "IPW")

# Remove the specified genes from the list
minusPWS_downregulated_df_with_intersection <- setdiff(minusPWS_downregulated_df_with_intersection, genes_to_remove)

# Output the updated list
minusPWS_downregulated_df_with_intersection

# Perform GO term analysis
go_results_withoutPWSgenes <- enrichGO(
  gene          = minusPWS_downregulated_df_with_intersection, # List of genes
  OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,      # Database for human genes
  keyType       = "SYMBOL",          # Format of gene names (e.g., SYMBOL)
  ont           = "ALL",             # Ontology: "BP" for Biological Process, "MF" for Molecular Function, "CC" for Cellular Component
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",              # Method for p-value adjustment
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,             # p-value threshold
  qvalueCutoff  = 0.05              # q-value threshold
)

# Display results
head(go_results_withoutPWSgenes)

# Bar plot of GO term results (without PWS locus genes)
barplot(go_results_withoutPWSgenes, showCategory = 20, title = "Bar Plot: GO Terms without PWS Locus Genes")  # Shows the top 20 categories

# Dot plot of GO term results (without PWS locus genes)
dotplot(go_results_withoutPWSgenes, showCategory = 20, title = "Dot Plot: GO Terms without PWS Locus Genes")  # Shows the top 20 categories

## Compare the results to the GO terms including the deleted PWS genes
# Perform GO term analysis
go_results_withPWSgenes <- enrichGO(
  gene          = downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection[["000011"]], # List of genes
  OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,      # Database for human genes
  keyType       = "SYMBOL",          # Format of gene names (e.g., SYMBOL)
  ont           = "ALL",             # Ontology: "BP" for Biological Process, "MF" for Molecular Function, "CC" for Cellular Component
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",              # Method for p-value adjustment
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,             # p-value threshold
  qvalueCutoff  = 0.05              # q-value threshold
)

# Bar plot of GO term results (with PWS genes)
barplot(go_results_withPWSgenes, showCategory = 20, title = "Bar Plot: GO Terms with PWS Genes")  # Shows the top 20 categories

# Dot plot of GO term results (with PWS genes)
dotplot(go_results_withPWSgenes, showCategory = 20, title = "Dot Plot: GO Terms with PWS Genes")  # Shows the top 20 categories


# Create a Venn diagram to overlap the GO terms with and without PWS genes
# Extract GO terms from each result
go_terms_withoutPWSgenes <- unique(go_results_withoutPWSgenes@result$ID)
go_terms_withPWSgenes <- unique(go_results_withPWSgenes@result$ID)

grid.newpage()

venn.plot <- venn.diagram(
  x = list(Analysis1 = go_terms_withoutPWSgenes, Analysis2 = go_terms_withPWSgenes),
  category.names = c("All GO Terms", "GO Terms with PWS Genes"),
  filename = NULL,
  output = TRUE,
  fill = c("#66c2a5", "#fc8d62"),
  alpha = 0.7,
  cex = 1.8,
  cat.cex = 1.8,
  cat.pos = c(270, 90), # 270 degrees (left), 90 degrees (right)
  cat.dist = c(0.05, 0.05),
  cat.fontfamily = "sans",
  cex.main = 2,
  main = "Comparison of GO Term Sets",
  main.fontfamily = "sans",
  fontfamily = "sans",
  margin = 0.1
)

grid.draw(venn.plot)

# Define the sets for the Venn diagram
venn_counts <- list(
  "GO terms of all downregulated PWS-specific DEGs" = go_terms_withoutPWSgenes,
  "GO terms of downregulated PWS-specific DEGs excluding the genes within the locus" = go_terms_withPWSgenes
)

# Generate the Euler diagram with counts
fit <- euler(venn_counts)

# Plot with counts displayed, using a simpler color scheme and no set names, labeled as a Euler diagram
plot(
  fit,
  fills = c("#D3D3D3", "#FFFFFF"),     # Simple green color scheme
  quantities = FALSE,                  # Display counts for each section
  labels = FALSE,                     # Remove set names
  edges = list(col = "grey30"),       # Edge color for a softer appearance
  main = "Euler Diagram: GO Term Overlap" # Added title via the 'main' argument
)

# Find overlapping terms
overlapping_terms <- intersect(go_terms_withoutPWSgenes, go_terms_withPWSgenes)

# Find terms unique to go_terms_withoutPWSgenes
unique_to_go_terms_withoutPWSgenes <- setdiff(go_terms_withoutPWSgenes, go_terms_withPWSgenes)

# Find terms unique to go_terms_withPWSgenes
unique_to_go_terms_withPWSgenes <- setdiff(go_terms_withPWSgenes, go_terms_withoutPWSgenes)

# Function to get GO term names
get_GO_names <- function(go_ids) {
  AnnotationDbi::select(GO.db, keys = go_ids, columns = "TERM", keytype = "GOID")
}

# Retrieve names for each category
overlapping_names <- get_GO_names(overlapping_terms)
unique_to_go_terms_withoutPWSgenes_names <- get_GO_names(unique_to_go_terms_withoutPWSgenes)
unique_to_go_terms_withPWSgenes_names <- get_GO_names(unique_to_go_terms_withPWSgenes)

# Display results as named lists
list(
  Overlapping = overlapping_names,
  Unique_to_go_terms_withoutPWSgenes = unique_to_go_terms_withoutPWSgenes_names,
  Unique_to_go_terms_withPWSgenes = unique_to_go_terms_withPWSgenes_names
)


# 1. Extract the results data frame
# Assuming 'go_results_withoutPWSgenes' is a loaded enrichment object
results_df <- go_results_withoutPWSgenes@result

# 2. Prepare the data for plotting
top_results_df <- results_df %>%
  # Convert GeneRatio from "numerator/denominator" text to a numeric value
  mutate(GeneRatio = sapply(strsplit(GeneRatio, "/"), function(x) as.numeric(x[1]) / as.numeric(x[2]))) %>%
  arrange(p.adjust) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15)

# 3. Recreate the dot plot with the new color gradient
ggplot(top_results_df,
       aes(x = GeneRatio,
           y = reorder(Description, GeneRatio),
           color = p.adjust,
           size = Count)) +
  geom_point() +
  # Use the specified two-color gradient
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "firebrick") +
  labs(
    title = "Dot Plot: GO Terms without PWS Locus Genes",
    x = "Gene Ratio",
    y = "GO Term",
    color = "p.adjust" # It's good practice to add the legend title here
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# 1. Split the slash-separated gene strings into a proper list of vectors
gene_lists <- strsplit(top_results_df$geneID, "/")

# 2. Assign the GO term descriptions as the names of the list items
names(gene_lists) <- top_results_df$Description

# 3. Output the list to see the genes for each term
gene_lists
```

11.2 Heatmap of all genes from GO terms of the PWS-specific dotplot

```{r}

# --- Prerequisite Checks ---
if (!exists("dds_RNA")) stop("Error: 'dds_RNA' object not found.")
if (!exists("top_results_df")) stop("Error: 'top_results_df' (GO results) not found.")

# ==============================================================================
# Step 1: Map Genes to Top 15 GO Terms
# ==============================================================================
message("Mapping genes to Top 15 GO Terms...")

# Extract genes from the slash-separated 'geneID' column
gene_lists_top15 <- strsplit(top_results_df$geneID, "/")
names(gene_lists_top15) <- top_results_df$Description

# Create a clean "Gene -> GO Term" dataframe
gene_to_go_map <- data.frame(
  Gene = character(),
  GO_Term = character(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (term in names(gene_lists_top15)) {
  genes <- gene_lists_top15[[term]]
  temp_df <- data.frame(Gene = genes, GO_Term = term)
  gene_to_go_map <- rbind(gene_to_go_map, temp_df)
}

# Deduplication: Assign gene to its *most significant* GO term (first occurrence)
# This prevents the same gene from appearing in multiple rows
gene_to_go_map <- gene_to_go_map[!duplicated(gene_to_go_map$Gene), ]
rownames(gene_to_go_map) <- gene_to_go_map$Gene

# ==============================================================================
# Step 2: Fetch Expression Data (VST)
# ==============================================================================
message("Fetching expression data for ", nrow(gene_to_go_map), " genes...")

# Intersect with available genes in the dds object
valid_genes <- intersect(rownames(gene_to_go_map), rownames(dds_RNA))

if (length(valid_genes) < 2) {
  stop("Error: Less than 2 genes found in dds_RNA. Check gene symbol consistency.")
}

# Fetch VST data (Variance Stabilizing Transformation)
vst_data <- vst(dds_RNA, blind = FALSE)
heatmap_matrix_raw <- assay(vst_data)[valid_genes, , drop = FALSE]

# ==============================================================================
# Step 3: Calculate Z-Scores Separately by Background
# ==============================================================================
message("Calculating Z-scores separately for MGH and 8330...")

# Get sample metadata
col_annotation_rna <- as.data.frame(colData(dds_RNA)[, c("background", "genotype")])

# Identify samples by background
samples_mgh  <- rownames(col_annotation_rna)[col_annotation_rna$background == "MGH"]
samples_8330 <- rownames(col_annotation_rna)[col_annotation_rna$background == "8330"]

# Split the matrix
matrix_mgh  <- heatmap_matrix_raw[, samples_mgh, drop = FALSE]
matrix_8330 <- heatmap_matrix_raw[, samples_8330, drop = FALSE]

# Function for Row-wise Z-score
cal_z_row <- function(mat) {
  # (Value - Mean) / SD
  if (ncol(mat) < 3) warning("Warning: Calculating Z-score with very few samples per group.")
  t(scale(t(mat)))
}

# Calculate Z-scores
z_mgh  <- cal_z_row(matrix_mgh)
z_8330 <- cal_z_row(matrix_8330)

# Recombine the matrices
z_matrix_combined <- cbind(z_mgh, z_8330)

# ==============================================================================
# Step 4: Sort Columns (Genotype > Background)
# ==============================================================================
message("Sorting columns by Genotype -> Background...")

# Define strict factor levels for sorting
col_annotation_rna$genotype <- factor(col_annotation_rna$genotype, 
                                      levels = c("DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", 
                                                 "PWSTI", "PWSTII", "WT"))

col_annotation_rna$background <- factor(col_annotation_rna$background, 
                                        levels = c("MGH", "8330"))

# Create sorted annotation dataframe
# Order: Genotype first, then Background within Genotype
col_annotation_sorted <- col_annotation_rna[order(col_annotation_rna$genotype, 
                                                  col_annotation_rna$background), ]

# Match matrix columns to this order
ordered_samples <- rownames(col_annotation_sorted)
heatmap_matrix_final <- z_matrix_combined[, ordered_samples]

# ==============================================================================
# Step 5: Prepare Colors and Annotations
# ==============================================================================
# Row Annotation (GO Terms)
row_annotation_go <- gene_to_go_map[valid_genes, "GO_Term", drop = FALSE]
colnames(row_annotation_go) <- "GO Term"

# Colors for GO Terms (Palette for 15 categories)
go_labels <- unique(row_annotation_go$`GO Term`)
get_distinct_colors <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Paired"))
go_colors <- setNames(get_distinct_colors(length(go_labels)), go_labels)

# Colors for Genotype/Background (Using your previous logic)
gg_color_hue <- function(n) {
  hues = seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
  hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100)[1:n]
}

genotype_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(levels(col_annotation_sorted$genotype))), 
                            levels(col_annotation_sorted$genotype))
background_colors <- setNames(gg_color_hue(length(levels(col_annotation_sorted$background))), 
                              levels(col_annotation_sorted$background))

ann_colors_final <- list(
  genotype = genotype_colors,
  background = background_colors,
  `GO Term` = go_colors
)

# ==============================================================================
# Step 6: Generate Final Heatmap
# ==============================================================================
message("Generating Heatmap...")

pheatmap(
  heatmap_matrix_final,
  
  # Annotations
  annotation_col = col_annotation_sorted,
  annotation_row = row_annotation_go,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors_final,
  
  # Clustering
  cluster_rows = TRUE,    # Cluster genes to see functional patterns
  cluster_cols = FALSE,   # STRICTLY respect our sorted column order
  
  # Appearance
  show_rownames = TRUE, 
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 6,       # Adjust based on number of genes
  fontsize_col = 8,
  
  # Color Scheme (Blue-White-Red)
  color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 10, name = "RdBu")))(100),
  main = "Expression of Top 15 GO Terms (Z-Score by Background)",
  scale = "none",         # Data is already Z-scored manually
  breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  border_color = NA
)

message("Done! Heatmap displayed.")
```

11.3 same for Ubi

```{r}
list_Ubi_up_overlap <-
  list(
    "overlap_DEU_DelC" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_DelC_up,
                                   list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_DelC_up),
    "overlap_DEU_DupC" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_DupC_up,
                                   list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_DupC_up),
    "overlap_DEU_HomoDel" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_HomoDel_up,
                                      list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_HomoDel_up),
    "overlap_DEU_PWSTI" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_PWSTI_up,
                                    list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_PWSTI_up),
    "overlap_DEU_PWSTII" = intersect(list_MGH_new_Ubi_up$Protein_PWSTII_up,
                                     list_8330_new_Ubi_up$Protein_PWSTII_up)
  )


upregulated_Ubionly <- list(
  DEG_DelC = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_DelC,
  DEG_DupC = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_DupC,
  DEG_HomoDel = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_HomoDel,
  DEG_PWSTI = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_PWSTI,
  DEG_PWSTII = list_Ubi_up_overlap$overlap_DEU_PWSTII
  )

upregulated_complex_Ubionly <- fromList(upregulated_Ubionly) == 1
upregulated_complex_Ubionly <- as.data.frame(upregulated_complex_Ubionly)

  
  
upregulated_Ubionly_df <- fromList(upregulated_Ubionly)
upregulated_Ubionly_df$gene <- unique(unlist(upregulated_Ubionly))

upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection <- upregulated_Ubionly_df %>%
  tidyr::unite(col = "intersection", -c("gene"), sep = "")

upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection <-
  upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection %>%
  group_by(intersection) %>%
  summarise(list = list(gene)) %>%
  mutate(list = setNames(list, intersection)) %>%
  pull(list)

#DelC-specific
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["10000"]]

#DupC-specific
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["01000"]]

#SYS-specific RNA
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["11000"]]

#HomoDel-specific
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["00100"]]

#PWSTI-specific
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["00010"]]

#PWSTII-specific
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["00001"]]

#PWS only
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["00011"]]

#all DelC-exclusive gene only
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["01111"]]

#all RNA
upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["11111"]]

```

11.4 Heatmap of PWS-specific ubiquitinated proteins

```{r}
# --- Prerequisite: Define the gene list and ensure expression data exists ---

# 1. Define your specific Ubiquitin target list (PWS-Only Upregulated)
target_genes <- upregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection[["00011"]]

# 2. Check if the Protein data objects exist (from Section 6.3)
if (!exists("Ubi_data_imp_MGH_new") || !exists("Ubi_data_imp_8330_new")) {
  stop("Please run Section 6.3 to load the Ubiquitin data objects first.")
}

message("Preparing expression data for the target Ubiquitin list...")

# --- STEP 0: Prepare the Expression Matrix for Target Proteins ---
# We need to extract the expression data for ONLY the 22 proteins in your list.
# We will pull from the raw data objects and re-calculate Z-scores to be safe.

# Extract raw data
exp_mgh <- assay(Ubi_data_imp_MGH_new)
exp_8330 <- assay(Ubi_data_imp_8330_new)

# Ensure valid row names (Gene Symbols) are available.
common_genes <- intersect(rownames(exp_mgh), rownames(exp_8330))
valid_targets <- intersect(target_genes, common_genes)

if (length(valid_targets) == 0) stop("None of the target proteins found in the data.")
message(sprintf("Found %d out of %d target proteins in the dataset.", length(valid_targets), length(target_genes)))

# Subset data
exp_mgh_sub <- exp_mgh[valid_targets, , drop=FALSE]
exp_8330_sub <- exp_8330[valid_targets, , drop=FALSE]

# Rename columns to ensure uniqueness
colnames(exp_mgh_sub) <- paste0("MGH_", colnames(exp_mgh_sub))
colnames(exp_8330_sub) <- paste0("B8330_", colnames(exp_8330_sub))

# Calculate Z-scores separately (Per background)
calc_z <- function(mat) {
  # Standard Z-score: (x - mean) / sd
  t(scale(t(mat)))
}
z_mgh <- calc_z(exp_mgh_sub)
z_8330 <- calc_z(exp_8330_sub)

# Combine
z_score_matrix_ubi <- cbind(z_mgh, z_8330)

# Cap Z-scores to +/- 2 for visualization
z_score_matrix_ubi[z_score_matrix_ubi > 2] <- 2
z_score_matrix_ubi[z_score_matrix_ubi < -2] <- -2

# --- STEP 1: Create "Functional Similarity" Matrix ---
# ==============================================================================
message("Retrieving GO terms for functional clustering...")

genes <- rownames(z_score_matrix_ubi)

# 1. Get all GO terms
go_df <- tryCatch({
  suppressMessages({
    AnnotationDbi::select(
      org.Hs.eg.db,
      keys = genes,
      columns = c("GO", "ONTOLOGY"),
      keytype = "SYMBOL"
    ) %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO))
  })
}, error = function(e) return(NULL))

if (is.null(go_df)) {
  warning("No GO terms found. Using 1 cluster.")
  hc_func <- hclust(dist(z_score_matrix_ubi)) 
  cluster_ids <- rep(1, length(genes))
  k_clusters <- 1
} else {
  # 2. Create Binary Matrix (Rows = Genes, Cols = GO Terms)
  go_table <- table(go_df$SYMBOL, go_df$GO)
  binary_matrix <- as.matrix.data.frame(as.data.frame.matrix(go_table))

  # Fill missing genes (those with no GO terms)
  missing_genes <- setdiff(genes, rownames(binary_matrix))
  if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
    zero_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = length(missing_genes), ncol = ncol(binary_matrix))
    rownames(zero_mat) <- missing_genes
    binary_matrix <- rbind(binary_matrix, zero_mat)
  }
  # Reorder to match expression matrix rows
  binary_matrix <- binary_matrix[genes, ]

  # ==============================================================================
  # STEP 2: Perform Functional Clustering
  # ==============================================================================
  dist_func <- dist(binary_matrix, method = "binary") # Jaccard distance
  hc_func <- hclust(dist_func, method = "ward.D2")
  
  # For 22 genes, 4 clusters is usually appropriate.
  k_clusters <- 8
  cluster_ids <- cutree(hc_func, k = k_clusters)
}

# ==============================================================================
# STEP 3: Label the Clusters (CORRECTED LOGIC)
# ==============================================================================
message(sprintf("Naming %d functional clusters using unique gene counts...", k_clusters))

# *** UPDATED FUNCTION: Counts unique genes, not raw annotations ***
get_cluster_bp_candidates <- function(genes) {
  go_subset <- go_df[go_df$SYMBOL %in% genes, ]
  if (nrow(go_subset) == 0) return("Unannotated")
  
  suppressMessages({
    go_terms <- AnnotationDbi::select(GO.db, keys = unique(go_subset$GO), columns = "TERM", keytype = "GOID")
  })
  
  # Merge to associate Terms with Genes
  merged_data <- merge(go_subset, go_terms, by.x="GO", by.y="GOID")
  
  # *** THE FIX: Unique pairs only (Gene + Term) ***
  unique_pairs <- unique(merged_data[, c("SYMBOL", "TERM")])
  
  # Count frequency based on UNIQUE genes
  term_counts <- sort(table(unique_pairs$TERM), decreasing = TRUE)
  
  return(names(term_counts))
}

# Re-run labeling loop with the fixed function
cluster_annotation <- data.frame(ClusterID = cluster_ids)
cluster_annotation$Function_Label <- NA
used_names <- c()
final_names <- character(k_clusters)

for (i in 1:k_clusters) {
  genes_in_cluster <- rownames(cluster_annotation)[cluster_annotation$ClusterID == i]
  candidates <- get_cluster_bp_candidates(genes_in_cluster)
  
  chosen_name <- NA
  # Try to pick the top candidate that hasn't been used yet
  for (cand in candidates) {
    cand_cap <- paste0(toupper(substr(cand, 1, 1)), substr(cand, 2, nchar(cand)))
    if (!cand_cap %in% used_names) {
      chosen_name <- cand_cap
      break
    }
  }
  
  # Fallback if all are taken
  if (is.na(chosen_name)) {
    base <- candidates[1]
    if(is.na(base)) base <- "Unknown Function"
    chosen_name <- paste0(toupper(substr(base, 1, 1)), substr(base, 2, nchar(base)), " (", i, ")")
  }
  
  final_names[i] <- chosen_name
  used_names <- c(used_names, chosen_name)
}

# Apply text wrapping
wrap_text <- function(x, width = 25) {
  sapply(x, function(s) paste(strwrap(s, width = width), collapse = "\n"))
}
final_names_wrapped <- wrap_text(final_names, width = 25)

for (i in 1:k_clusters) {
  cluster_annotation$Function_Label[cluster_annotation$ClusterID == i] <- final_names_wrapped[i]
}

# ==============================================================================
# STEP 4: Generate Heatmap (With Corrected Labels)
# ==============================================================================

# Update the Row Annotation object with new labels
row_annotation_final <- cluster_annotation[, "Function_Label", drop = FALSE]
colnames(row_annotation_final) <- "Biological Process"
rownames(row_annotation_final) <- rownames(z_score_matrix_ubi)

# Create column annotation from sample origin
col_annotation <- data.frame(
  Background = ifelse(grepl("^MGH_", colnames(z_score_matrix_ubi)), "MGH", "B8330")
)
rownames(col_annotation) <- colnames(z_score_matrix_ubi)

# Update colors
labels <- unique(row_annotation_final$`Biological Process`)
role_colors <- setNames(
  brewer.pal(n = max(length(labels), 3), name = "Dark2")[1:length(labels)],
  labels
)

ann_colors_final <- list(
  Background = c(
    MGH = "#1B9E77",
    B8330 = "#D95F02"
  ),
  `Biological Process` = role_colors
)

message("Generating Corrected Ubiquitin Heatmap...")

pheatmap_ubi_func <- pheatmap(
  z_score_matrix_ubi,
  
  annotation_col = col_annotation,
  annotation_row = row_annotation_final,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors_final,
  
  cluster_rows = hc_func,
  cutree_rows = k_clusters,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  
  color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 10, name = "RdBu")))(100),
  main = "PWS-Only Ubiquitinated Proteins: Clustered by Function\n(Labels match Unique Gene Count)",
  scale = "none",
  breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  border_color = NA
)

print(pheatmap_ubi_func)
```

12.1 Cell type enrichment - RNA

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 3.B - GENE SET ENRICHMENT (MSigDB C8: CELL TYPE SIGNATURES)
# ==============================================================================

# --- Step 1: Load Libraries and Prepare C8 Gene Sets ---
library(msigdbr)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(dplyr)

message("Fetching C8 cell type signature gene sets from MSigDB...")

# Fetch all human gene sets from the C8 collection
c8_gene_sets_raw <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "C8")

# Format the data for clusterProfiler using GENE SYMBOLS
c8_term2gene <- c8_gene_sets_raw %>%
  dplyr::select(gs_name, gene_symbol) %>%
  as.data.frame()

message("C8 gene sets prepared successfully.")

# --- Step 2: Run C8 Enrichment for MGH RNA-seq Data ---

# MGH - Upregulated Genes
C8_list_MGH_RNA_up <- list()
for (comp in names(DEG_list_MGH)) {
  signif_genes <- DEG_list_MGH[[comp]][DEG_list_MGH[[comp]][, "padj"] < 0.05 &
                                         !is.na(DEG_list_MGH[[comp]][, "padj"]) &
                                          DEG_list_MGH[[comp]][, "log2FoldChange"] > 0, ] %>%
    rownames()
  
  c8_enrich_results <- enricher(
    gene = signif_genes,
    TERM2GENE = c8_term2gene, # Use the C8 gene sets
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff  = 0.05
  )
  C8_list_MGH_RNA_up[[comp]] <- c8_enrich_results
}

# MGH - Downregulated Genes
C8_list_MGH_RNA_down <- list()
for (comp in names(DEG_list_MGH)) {
  signif_genes <- DEG_list_MGH[[comp]][DEG_list_MGH[[comp]][, "padj"] < 0.05 &
                                         !is.na(DEG_list_MGH[[comp]][, "padj"]) &
                                          DEG_list_MGH[[comp]][, "log2FoldChange"] < 0, ] %>%
    rownames()
    
  c8_enrich_results <- enricher(
    gene = signif_genes,
    TERM2GENE = c8_term2gene,
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff  = 0.05
  )
  C8_list_MGH_RNA_down[[comp]] <- c8_enrich_results
}

# --- Step 3: Run C8 Enrichment for 8330 RNA-seq Data ---

# 8330 - Upregulated Genes
C8_list_8330_RNA_up <- list()
for (comp in names(DEG_list_8330)) {
  signif_genes <- DEG_list_8330[[comp]][DEG_list_8330[[comp]][, "padj"] < 0.05 &
                                          !is.na(DEG_list_8330[[comp]][, "padj"]) &
                                           DEG_list_8330[[comp]][, "log2FoldChange"] > 0, ] %>%
    rownames()
    
  c8_enrich_results <- enricher(
    gene = signif_genes,
    TERM2GENE = c8_term2gene,
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff  = 0.05
  )
  C8_list_8330_RNA_up[[comp]] <- c8_enrich_results
}

# 8330 - Downregulated Genes
C8_list_8330_RNA_down <- list()
for (comp in names(DEG_list_8330)) {
  signif_genes <- DEG_list_8330[[comp]][DEG_list_8330[[comp]][, "padj"] < 0.05 &
                                          !is.na(DEG_list_8330[[comp]][, "padj"]) &
                                           DEG_list_8330[[comp]][, "log2FoldChange"] < 0, ] %>%
    rownames()
    
  c8_enrich_results <- enricher(
    gene = signif_genes,
    TERM2GENE = c8_term2gene,
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff  = 0.05
  )
  C8_list_8330_RNA_down[[comp]] <- c8_enrich_results
}

# --- Step 4: Assign Final Names to the Result Lists ---
names(C8_list_MGH_RNA_up)    <- names(DEG_list_MGH)
names(C8_list_MGH_RNA_down)  <- names(DEG_list_MGH)

names(C8_list_8330_RNA_up)   <- names(DEG_list_8330)
names(C8_list_8330_RNA_down) <- names(DEG_list_8330)

message("C8 cell type signature enrichment analysis complete for all RNA-seq comparisons.")

```

12.2 Filtering

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 3.C - MERGE C8 ENRICHMENT RESULTS INTO A SINGLE DATAFRAME
# ==============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

# Helper function to extract results and add metadata
extract_enrich_results <- function(enrich_list, direction, background) {
  # Filter out NULL elements (comparisons with no significant genes)
  valid_results <- Filter(Negate(is.null), enrich_list)
  
  map_dfr(names(valid_results), function(comp_name) {
    res_df <- valid_results[[comp_name]]@result
    
    # Add metadata columns required for plotting
    res_df %>%
      mutate(
        comparison = comp_name,
        direction = direction,
        background = background
      )
  })
}

# Combine the 4 lists from Step 3.B
results_1 <- extract_enrich_results(C8_list_MGH_RNA_up,    "Upregulated",   "MGH")
results_2 <- extract_enrich_results(C8_list_MGH_RNA_down,  "Downregulated", "MGH")
results_3 <- extract_enrich_results(C8_list_8330_RNA_up,   "Upregulated",   "8330")
results_4 <- extract_enrich_results(C8_list_8330_RNA_down, "Downregulated", "8330")

# Create the final master dataframe
all_c8_results <- bind_rows(results_1, results_2, results_3, results_4)

message("Merged 'all_c8_results' created successfully. Rows: ", nrow(all_c8_results))
```

12.3 Visualization based on Zhong Data

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 3.F - VISUALIZE A SECOND SPECIFIC SUBSET OF C8 ENRICHMENT RESULTS
# ==============================================================================

# --- Step 1: Define Your Second Custom List of Cell Type Signatures ---
specific_cell_types_list_2 <- c(
  "ZHONG_PFC_C1_ASTROCYTE",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C1_DLX5_POS_INTERNEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C1_MICROGLIA",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C1_NEUROD1_POS_EXCITATORY_NEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C1_OPC",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C2_ASCL1_POS_ASTROCYTE",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C2_SOX5_BCL11B_POS_EXCITATORY_NEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C2_THY1_POS_OPC",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C2_UNKNOWN_NPC",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C3_ASTROCYTE",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C3_HOPX_POS_OPC",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C3_MICROGLIA",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C3_PROX1_CCK_POS_INTERNEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C3_UNKNOWN_INP",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C4_PTGDS_POS_OPC",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C4_UNKNOWN_INP",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C5_BCL11B_CALB2_POS_INP",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C6_DLX5_GAD1_GAD2_POS_INTERNEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C6_PLXNA4_POS_EXCITATORY_NEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C7_ORG_UNDERGOING_NEURONAL_DIFFERENTIATION",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C7_SST_LHX6_POS_PUTATIVE_MIGRATING_INTERNEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C8_ORG_PROLIFERATING",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C8_UNKNOWN_NEUROD2_POS_INTERNEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C9_ORG_OTHER",
  "ZHONG_PFC_HES1_POS_C1_NPC",
  "ZHONG_PFC_MAJOR_TYPES_ASTROCYTES",
  "ZHONG_PFC_MAJOR_TYPES_EXCITATORY_NEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_MAJOR_TYPES_INTERNEURON",
  "ZHONG_PFC_MAJOR_TYPES_MICROGLIA",
  "ZHONG_PFC_MAJOR_TYPES_NPCS",
  "ZHONG_PFC_MAJOR_TYPES_OPC"
)

# --- Step 2: Filter the Full Results and Prepare for Plotting ---
# This uses the 'all_c8_results' data frame created in the previous step.
plot_data_subset_2 <- all_c8_results %>%
  filter(Description %in% specific_cell_types_list_2) %>%
  mutate(
    negLog10pAdj = -log10(p.adjust + 1e-10),
    condition = paste0(comparison, "_", background)
  )

# --- Step 3: Create the Combined Dot Plot ---
if(nrow(plot_data_subset_2) > 0) {
  subset_dotplot_2 <- ggplot(plot_data_subset_2, aes(x = condition, y = fct_reorder(Description, negLog10pAdj, .fun = max))) +
    geom_point(aes(size = Count, color = negLog10pAdj)) +
    facet_grid(. ~ direction, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
    scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "firebrick") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8)) +
    labs(
      title = "Enrichment of Specific Prefrontal Cortex (PFC) Cell Types",
      x = "Condition",
      y = "Cell Type Signature Gene Set",
      color = bquote(-Log[10]~"(p-adj)"),
      size = "Gene Count"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  print(subset_dotplot_2)

} else {
  message("None of the specified PFC cell types were found among the significant enrichment results.")
}
```

```{r}
# --- Step 2: Filter the Full Results and Prepare for Plotting ---

plot_data_subset_1 <- all_c8_results %>%

    negLog10pAdj = -log10(p.adjust + 1e-10),
    condition = paste0(comparison, "_", background)

# --- Step 3: Create the Combined Dot Plot ---

if(nrow(plot_data_subset_1) > 0) {

  subset_dotplot_1 <- ggplot(plot_data_subset_1, aes(x = condition, y = fct_reorder(Description, negLog10pAdj, .fun = max))) +
    geom_point(aes(size = Count, color = negLog10pAdj)) +
    facet_grid(. ~ direction, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
    scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "firebrick") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8)) +
    labs(
      title = "Enrichment of Specific Embryonic Cortex Cell Types",
      x = "Condition",
      y = "Cell Type Signature Gene Set",
      color = bquote(-Log[10]~"(p-adj)"),
      size = "Gene Count"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  print(subset_dotplot_1)

} else {
  message("None of the specified cell types were found among the significant enrichment results.")
}
```

12.5 Selected neuronal cell types

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 3.E - VISUALIZE A SPECIFIC SUBSET OF C8 ENRICHMENT RESULTS (CUSTOM ORDER)
# ==============================================================================

# --- Step 1: Define Your Custom List of Cell Type Signatures ---
specific_cell_types_list_3 <- c(
  "ZHONG_PFC_C8_ORG_PROLIFERATING",
  "FAN_EMBRYONIC_CTX_NSC_2",
  "ZHONG_PFC_MAJOR_TYPES_NPCS",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C3_UNKNOWN_INP",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C7_ORG_UNDERGOING_NEURONAL_DIFFERENTIATION",
  "ZHONG_PFC_C8_UNKNOWN_NEUROD2_POS_INTERNEURON",
  "FAN_EMBRYONIC_CTX_EX_4_EXCITATORY_NEURON"
)

# --- Step 2: Filter the Full Results ---
plot_data_subset_3 <- all_c8_results %>%
  filter(Description %in% specific_cell_types_list_3) %>%
  mutate(
    negLog10pAdj = -log10(p.adjust + 1e-10),
    condition = paste0(comparison, "_", background)
  )

# --- Step 3: Set the Custom Factor Order ---
# This is the key step. We convert the 'Description' column to a factor
# and tell it to use the order from your list.
# We use rev() because ggplot plots the first level at the bottom.
if (nrow(plot_data_subset_3) > 0) {
  plot_data_subset_3$Description <- factor(
    plot_data_subset_3$Description, 
    levels = rev(specific_cell_types_list_3)
  )
}

# --- Step 4: Create the Combined Dot Plot ---
if (nrow(plot_data_subset_3) > 0) {

  subset_dotplot_3 <- ggplot(
    plot_data_subset_3, 
    # The y aesthetic is now just 'Description', as its order is already set.
    aes(x = condition, y = Description)
  ) +
    geom_point(aes(size = Count, color = negLog10pAdj)) +
    facet_grid(. ~ direction, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
    scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "firebrick") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8)) +
    labs(
      title = "Enrichment of Specified Neurodevelopmental Cell Types",
      x = "Condition",
      y = "Cell Type Signature Gene Set",
      color = bquote(-Log[10]~"(p-adj)"),
      size = "Gene Count"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  print(subset_dotplot_3)

} else {
  message("None of the specified cell types were found among the significant enrichment results.")
}
```

14.1 Export of DEG lists compared to WT

```{r}
if (exists("dds_RNA") && is(dds_RNA, "DESeqDataSet")) {
    available_gois <- sort(unique(rownames(dds_RNA)))
  } else { available_gois <- c("Transcriptome Data Error"); data_loaded_flag <- FALSE }
  
  if (exists("dds_RNA") && is(dds_RNA, "DESeqDataSet") && "background" %in% colnames(colData(dds_RNA))) {
    dds_RNA_8330 <- dds_RNA[, dds_RNA$background == "8330"]; dds_RNA_MGH <- dds_RNA[, dds_RNA$background == "MGH"]
    colData(dds_RNA_8330)$condition <- colData(dds_RNA_8330)$genotype; colData(dds_RNA_MGH)$condition <- colData(dds_RNA_MGH)$genotype
    master_condition_order <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII", "WT")
    available_conditions <- master_condition_order
    rna_data_prepared <- TRUE
  }

# --- 2. START THE SCRIPT LOGIC ---
tryCatch({
  
  # Check if data objects exist
  if (!exists("dds_RNA_8330") || !exists("DEG_list_8330") || !exists("dds_RNA_MGH") || !exists("DEG_list_MGH")) {
    stop("Error: Required data objects (dds_RNA_..., DEG_list_...) not found.
         Please ensure the main data loading script has been run.")
  }

  # 3. CREATE A NEW LIST FOR ALL SHEETS
  # The order we add to this list is the order they will appear in Excel
  all_export_sheets <- list()

  # 4. ADD THE "ALL COUNTS" SHEETS FIRST
  
  # --- MGH Normalized Counts (Sheet 1) ---
  message("Preparing 'MGH_Normalized_Counts' sheet...")
  mgh_counts_df <- as.data.frame(counts(dds_RNA_MGH, normalized = TRUE))
  mgh_counts_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(mgh_counts_df, "Gene")
  all_export_sheets[["MGH_Normalized_Counts"]] <- mgh_counts_df

  # --- 8330 Normalized Counts (Sheet 2) ---
  message("Preparing '8330_Normalized_Counts' sheet...")
  e8330_counts_df <- as.data.frame(counts(dds_RNA_8330, normalized = TRUE))
  e8330_counts_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(e8330_counts_df, "Gene")
  all_export_sheets[["8330_Normalized_Counts"]] <- e8330_counts_df
  
  
  # 5. PROCESS AND ADD THE DEG COMPARISON SHEETS
  
  # --- MGH DEG Sheets (Sheets 3, 4, ...) ---
  message("Processing MGH DEG comparison sheets (removing baseMean)...")
  for (comp_name in names(DEG_list_MGH)) {
    sheet_name <- paste0("MGH_", comp_name)
    
    # Convert to data frame and add Gene column
    df <- as.data.frame(DEG_list_MGH[[comp_name]])
    df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(df, "Gene")
    
    # --- KEY CHANGE: Remove the baseMean column ---
    if ("baseMean" %in% colnames(df)) {
      df <- dplyr::select(df, -baseMean)
    }
    
    # Add the modified data frame to our list
    all_export_sheets[[sheet_name]] <- df
  }
  
  # --- 8330 DEG Sheets (Sheets ...) ---
  message("Processing 8330 DEG comparison sheets (removing baseMean)...")
  for (comp_name in names(DEG_list_8330)) {
    sheet_name <- paste0("8330_", comp_name)
    
    # Convert to data frame and add Gene column
    df <- as.data.frame(DEG_list_8330[[comp_name]])
    df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(df, "Gene")
    
    # --- KEY CHANGE: Remove the baseMean column ---
     if ("baseMean" %in% colnames(df)) {
      df <- dplyr::select(df, -baseMean)
    }
    
    # Add the modified data frame to our list
    all_export_sheets[[sheet_name]] <- df
  }
  
  # 6. WRITE THE COMBINED LIST TO A SINGLE .XLSX FILE
  output_filename <- "DEG_summary_and_All_Counts.xlsx"
  message(paste("\nWriting all", length(all_export_sheets), "sheets to:", output_filename, "..."))
  
  write.xlsx(
    all_export_sheets, 
    file = output_filename, 
    rowNames = FALSE 
  )
  
  # 7. PRINT A CONFIRMATION MESSAGE
  message("Successfully exported all results.")
  message(paste("The file", output_filename, "has been created with normalized counts on the first two sheets."))
  
}, error = function(e) {
  message(paste("!!! An error occurred:", e$message, "!!!"))
})
```

14.2 Export of DEP lists compared to WT

```{r}
# --- 2. START THE SCRIPT LOGIC ---
tryCatch({
  
  # Check if all required data objects exist
  if (!exists("new_data_imp_MGH") || !exists("new_data_imp_8330") || 
      !exists("dep_MGH") || !exists("dep_8330")) {
    stop("Error: Required data objects (new_data_imp_..., dep_...) not found.
         Please ensure the main data loading script (STEP 2) has been run.")
  }

  # 3. CREATE A NEW LIST FOR ALL SHEETS
  # The order we add to this list is the order they will appear in Excel
  all_dep_sheets <- list()

  # 4. ADD THE "ALL ABUNDANCE" SHEETS FIRST
  
  # --- MGH Normalized Abundance (Sheet 1) ---
  message("Preparing 'MGH_Proteome_Abundance' sheet...")
  mgh_abundance_df <- as.data.frame(assay(new_data_imp_MGH))
  mgh_rowdata_df <- as.data.frame(rowData(new_data_imp_MGH))
  # Combine row identifiers and abundance data
  all_dep_sheets[["MGH_Proteome_Abundance"]] <- cbind(mgh_rowdata_df, mgh_abundance_df)

  # --- 8330 Normalized Abundance (Sheet 2) ---
  message("Preparing '8330_Proteome_Abundance' sheet...")
  e8330_abundance_df <- as.data.frame(assay(new_data_imp_8330))
  e8330_rowdata_df <- as.data.frame(rowData(new_data_imp_8330))
  # Combine row identifiers and abundance data
  all_dep_sheets[["8330_Proteome_Abundance"]] <- cbind(e8330_rowdata_df, e8330_abundance_df)
  
  
  # 5. PROCESS AND ADD THE DIFFERENTIAL PROTEOMICS (DEP) SHEETS
  
  # --- Re-using your helper function from the prompt ---
  mutant_comparisons <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HetDel", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")
  
  process_proteomics_results <- function(results_object, background_name) {
    # This function is from your prompt and works as intended.
    # It extracts comparison-specific columns, which already
    # excludes any "baseMean"-like global columns.
    results_df <- as.data.frame(rowData(results_object))
    sheets_list <- list()
    
    for (mutant in mutant_comparisons) {
      comparison_pattern <- paste0(mutant, "_vs_WT")
      sheet_name <- paste0(background_name, "_", comparison_pattern)
      
      # This select statement correctly grabs only identifiers
      # and columns for the specific comparison.
      sheet_data <- results_df %>%
        dplyr::select(
          any_of(c("name", "PG.ProteinGroups", "PG.Genes")), # Use any_of for safety
          starts_with(comparison_pattern)
        )
      
      sheets_list[[sheet_name]] <- sheet_data
    }
    return(sheets_list)
  }
  
  # --- Run the function for MGH and 8330 ---
  message("Processing MGH DEP comparison sheets...")
  # Using 'dep_MGH' (from STEP 2 loading)
  mgh_sheets <- process_proteomics_results(dep_MGH, "MGH") 
  
  message("Processing 8330 DEP comparison sheets...")
  # Using 'dep_8330' (from STEP 2 loading)
  b8330_sheets <- process_proteomics_results(dep_8330, "8330") 

  # --- Combine the lists ---
  # Appends the DEP sheets *after* the abundance sheets
  all_dep_sheets <- c(all_dep_sheets, mgh_sheets, b8330_sheets)

  
  # 6. WRITE THE COMBINED LIST TO A SINGLE .XLSX FILE
  output_filename <- "DEP_summary_and_All_Abundance.xlsx"
  message(paste("\nWriting all", length(all_dep_sheets), "sheets to:", output_filename, "..."))
  
  write.xlsx(
    all_dep_sheets, 
    file = output_filename,
    rowNames = FALSE
  )
  
  # 7. PRINT A CONFIRMATION MESSAGE
  message("Successfully exported all results.")
  message(paste("The file", output_filename, "has been created with normalized abundance on the first two sheets."))
  
}, error = function(e) {
  message(paste("!!! An error occurred:", e$message, "!!!"))
})
```

```{r}
tryCatch({
  
  # Check if all required data objects exist from STEP 2
  if (!exists("Ubi_data_imp_MGH") || !exists("Ubi_data_imp_8330") || 
      !exists("Ubi_dep_MGH") || !exists("Ubi_dep_8330")) {
    stop("Error: Required data objects (Ubi_data_imp_..., Ubi_dep_...) not found.
         Please ensure the main data loading script (STEP 2) has been run.")
  }

  # 3. CREATE A NEW LIST FOR ALL SHEETS
  all_deu_sheets <- list()

  # 4. ADD THE "ALL ABUNDANCE" SHEETS FIRST
  
  # --- MGH Normalized Ubiquitome Abundance (Sheet 1) ---
  message("Preparing 'MGH_Ubiquitome_Abundance' sheet...")
  mgh_ubi_abundance_df <- as.data.frame(assay(Ubi_data_imp_MGH))
  mgh_ubi_rowdata_df <- as.data.frame(rowData(Ubi_data_imp_MGH))
  all_deu_sheets[["MGH_Ubiquitome_Abundance"]] <- cbind(mgh_ubi_rowdata_df, mgh_ubi_abundance_df)

  # --- 8330 Normalized Ubiquitome Abundance (Sheet 2) ---
  message("Preparing '8330_Ubiquitome_Abundance' sheet...")
  e8330_ubi_abundance_df <- as.data.frame(assay(Ubi_data_imp_8330))
  e8330_ubi_rowdata_df <- as.data.frame(rowData(Ubi_data_imp_8330))
  all_deu_sheets[["8330_Ubiquitome_Abundance"]] <- cbind(e8330_ubi_rowdata_df, e8330_ubi_abundance_df)
  
  
  # 5. PROCESS AND ADD THE DIFFERENTIAL UBIQUITINOMICS (DEU) SHEETS
  
  # Using your helper function, as it already selects only comparison data
  mutant_comparisons_ubi <- c("DelC", "DupC", "HomoDel", "PWSTI", "PWSTII")
  
  process_ubiquitinomics_results <- function(results_object, background_name) {
    results_df <- as.data.frame(rowData(results_object))
    sheets_list <- list()
    
    for (mutant in mutant_comparisons_ubi) {
      comparison_pattern <- paste0(mutant, "_vs_WT")
      sheet_name <- paste0(background_name, "_", comparison_pattern)
      
      # This select statement correctly grabs only identifiers
      # and columns for the specific comparison (no baseMean)
      sheet_data <- results_df %>%
        dplyr::select(
          any_of(c("name", "PG.ProteinGroups", "PG.Genes")),
          starts_with(comparison_pattern)
        )
      
      sheets_list[[sheet_name]] <- sheet_data
    }
    return(sheets_list)
  }
  
  # --- Run the function for MGH and 8330 ---
  message("Processing MGH DEU comparison sheets...")
  # Using 'Ubi_dep_MGH' (from STEP 2 loading)
  mgh_sheets_ubi <- process_ubiquitinomics_results(Ubi_dep_MGH, "MGH") 
  
  message("Processing 8330 DEU comparison sheets...")
  # Using 'Ubi_dep_8330' (from STEP 2 loading)
  b8330_sheets_ubi <- process_ubiquitinomics_results(Ubi_dep_8330, "8330") 

  # --- Combine the lists ---
  # Appends the DEU sheets *after* the abundance sheets
  all_deu_sheets <- c(all_deu_sheets, mgh_sheets_ubi, b8330_sheets_ubi)

  
  # 6. WRITE THE COMBINED LIST TO A SINGLE .XLSX FILE
  output_filename <- "DEU_summary_and_All_Abundance.xlsx"
  message(paste("\nWriting all", length(all_deu_sheets), "sheets to:", output_filename, "..."))
  
  write.xlsx(
    all_deu_sheets, 
    file = output_filename,
    rowNames = FALSE
  )
  
  # 7. PRINT A CONFIRMATION MESSAGE
  message("Successfully exported all results.")
  message(paste("The file", output_filename, "has been created with normalized abundance on the first two sheets."))
  
}, error = function(e) {
  message(paste("!!! An error occurred:", e$message, "!!!"))
})
```

14.4 Export of DEG GO lists compared to WT

```{r}
# 1. Create a list to hold all the GO result data frames for export
all_go_rna_sheets <- list()

# 2. Combine the four GO result lists into a single master list for easier processing.
#    We also give them names to identify their origin (background and regulation).
list_of_go_lists <- list(
  "MGH_up"   = GO_list_MGH_RNA_up,
  "MGH_down" = GO_list_MGH_RNA_down,
  "8330_up"  = GO_list_8330_RNA_up,
  "8330_down"= GO_list_8330_RNA_down
)

# 3. Loop through the master list to process each of the four result sets
for (list_name in names(list_of_go_lists)) {
  
  # Get the current list of GO results (e.g., GO_list_MGH_RNA_up)
  current_go_list <- list_of_go_lists[[list_name]]
  
  # Loop through each comparison within that list (e.g., DelC, DupC, etc.)
  for (comp_name in names(current_go_list)) {
    
    # Create a clear, descriptive name for the Excel sheet
    # e.g., "MGH_RNA_DelC_up"
    sheet_name <- gsub("_vs_WT", "", paste0(list_name, "_", comp_name))
    
    # Extract the results data frame from the enrichResult object
    # The '@' symbol is used to access slots in S4 objects like these
    results_df <- as.data.frame(current_go_list[[comp_name]]@result)
    
    # Check if any significant GO terms were found.
    # If not, create a placeholder data frame with a message.
    if (nrow(results_df) == 0) {
      results_df <- data.frame(Message = "No significant GO terms were found for this comparison.")
    }
    
    # Add the prepared data frame to our final export list
    all_go_rna_sheets[[sheet_name]] <- results_df
  }
}

# 4. Write the combined list to a single .xlsx file
# Each data frame in 'all_go_rna_sheets' will become its own worksheet.
write.xlsx(
  all_go_rna_sheets, 
  file = "GO_results_RNA_summary.xlsx"
)

# 5. Print a confirmation message to the console
message("Successfully exported all RNA-seq GO term results to 'GO_results_RNA_summary.xlsx'")
message(paste("The file contains", length(all_go_rna_sheets), "sheets."))
```

14.6 Export of DEU GO lists compared to WT

```{r}
# 1. Create a list to hold all the GO result data frames for export
all_go_ubi_sheets <- list()

# 2. Combine the four ubiquitinomics GO result lists into a single master list.
list_of_ubi_go_lists <- list(
  "MGH_Ubi_up"   = GO_list_MGH_Ubi_up,
  "MGH_Ubi_down" = GO_list_MGH_Ubi_down,
  "8330_Ubi_up"  = GO_list_8330_Ubi_up,
  "8330_Ubi_down"= GO_list_8330_Ubi_down
)

# 3. Loop through the master list to process each of the four result sets
for (list_name in names(list_of_ubi_go_lists)) {
  
  current_go_list <- list_of_ubi_go_lists[[list_name]]
  
  for (comp_name in names(current_go_list)) {
    
    # --- MODIFIED PART ---
    # Create a shorter, cleaner name for the Excel sheet by removing redundant parts.
    # e.g., "MGH_Ubi_up" + "Protein_DelC_up" -> "MGH_Ubi_DelC_up"
    clean_comp <- gsub("Protein_", "", comp_name) # Removes "Protein_"
    clean_comp <- gsub("_up|_down", "", clean_comp) # Removes direction
    sheet_name <- paste0(list_name, "_", clean_comp) # Creates the final name

    # Extract the results data frame
    results_df <- as.data.frame(current_go_list[[comp_name]]@result)
    
    # Handle cases with no results
    if (nrow(results_df) == 0) {
      results_df <- data.frame(Message = "No significant GO terms were found for this comparison.")
    }
    
    all_go_ubi_sheets[[sheet_name]] <- results_df
  }
}

# 4. Write the combined list to a single .xlsx file
write.xlsx(
  all_go_ubi_sheets, 
  file = "GO_results_Ubiquitinomics_summary.xlsx"
)

# 5. Print a confirmation message
message("Successfully exported all Ubiquitinomics GO term results to 'GO_results_Ubiquitinomics_summary.xlsx'")
message(paste("The file contains", length(all_go_ubi_sheets), "sheets."))
```

14.7 Export of GO Upset Plot Intersections - Transcriptome

```{r}
# 1. Define the descriptive names for each intersection's binary code.
#    This map will be used to create the column headers in the Excel file.
intersection_name_map <- c(
  "100000" = "DelC_specific",
  "010000" = "DupC_specific",
  "001000" = "HetDel_specific",
  "000100" = "HomoDel_specific",
  "000010" = "PWSTI_specific",
  "000001" = "PWSTII_specific",
  "110000" = "SYS_only (DelC_DupC)",
  "001100" = "MAGEL2_del_only (HetDel_HomoDel)",
  "000011" = "PWS_only (PWSTI_PWSTII)",
  "001111" = "All_Deletions (HetDel_HomoDel_PWSTI_PWSTII)",
  "011111" = "All_except_DelC",
  "111111" = "Shared_in_All"
)

# 2. Create a helper function to format the lists for Excel.
#    Since each intersection has a different number of GO terms, this function
#    pads the shorter lists with 'NA' to create a rectangular data frame.
format_intersections_for_export <- function(intersection_list, name_map) {
  
  # Keep only the intersections that actually have GO terms
  list_to_process <- intersection_list[names(intersection_list) %in% names(name_map)]
  
  # Check if there's anything to process
  if (length(list_to_process) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(Message = "No intersections with the specified names were found."))
  }
  
  # Use the map to assign the new, descriptive names
  names(list_to_process) <- name_map[names(list_to_process)]
  
  # Find the maximum number of GO terms in any single intersection
  max_length <- max(sapply(list_to_process, length))
  
  # Pad each list of GO terms with NAs to match the max length
  padded_list <- lapply(list_to_process, function(terms) {
    c(terms, rep(NA, max_length - length(terms)))
  })
  
  # Convert the list of equal-length vectors into a data frame
  as.data.frame(padded_list)
}

# 3. Apply the function to both the upregulated and downregulated intersection lists
df_upregulated_named <- format_intersections_for_export(
  GO_upregulated_onlyRNA_df_with_intersection, 
  intersection_name_map
)

df_downregulated_named <- format_intersections_for_export(
  GO_downregulated_onlyRNA_df_with_intersection, 
  intersection_name_map
)

# 4. Write the new data frames to a single Excel file
list_for_export_named <- list(
  "Upregulated_RNA_GO_Intersects" = df_upregulated_named,
  "Downregulated_RNA_GO_Intersects" = df_downregulated_named
)

write.xlsx(
  list_for_export_named, 
  file = "GO_Upset_Intersections_RNA_Named.xlsx"
)

# 5. Print a confirmation message
message("Successfully exported named RNA GO Upset intersections to 'GO_Upset_Intersections_RNA_Named.xlsx'")
```

```{r}
  "001100" = "MAGEL2_del_Protein_only (HetDel_HomoDel)",
  "000011" = "PWS_Protein_only (PWSTI_PWSTII)",
  "001111" = "All_Deletions_Protein (HetDel_HomoDel_PWSTI_PWSTII)",
  "011111" = "All_except_DelC_Protein",
  "111111" = "Shared_in_All_Protein"
)

# 2. Reuse the helper function to format the lists for Excel.
#    (This function is identical to the one from the previous RNA export)
format_intersections_for_export <- function(intersection_list, name_map) {
  list_to_process <- intersection_list[names(intersection_list) %in% names(name_map)]
  if (length(list_to_process) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(Message = "No intersections with the specified names were found."))
  }
  names(list_to_process) <- name_map[names(list_to_process)]
  max_length <- max(sapply(list_to_process, length))
  padded_list <- lapply(list_to_process, function(terms) {
    c(terms, rep(NA, max_length - length(terms)))
  })
  as.data.frame(padded_list)
}

# 3. Apply the function to both the upregulated and downregulated protein intersection lists
df_upregulated_prot_named <- format_intersections_for_export(
  GO_upregulated_onlyProtein_df_with_intersection, 
  intersection_name_map_protein
)

df_downregulated_prot_named <- format_intersections_for_export(
  GO_downregulated_onlyProtein_df_with_intersection, 
  intersection_name_map_protein
)

# 4. Write the new data frames to a single Excel file
list_for_export_prot_named <- list(
  "Upregulated_Prot_GO_Inter" = df_upregulated_prot_named,
  "Downregulated_Prot_GO_Inter" = df_downregulated_prot_named
)

write.xlsx(
  list_for_export_prot_named, 
  file = "GO_Upset_Intersections_Protein_Named.xlsx"
)

# 5. Print a confirmation message
message("Successfully exported named Protein GO Upset intersections to 'GO_Upset_Intersections_Protein_Named.xlsx'")
```

14.11 Export of enriched disease database genes

```{r}
tryCatch({
  
  # --- 1. Check Requirements ---
  required_objects <- c("all_enrichment_data", "gene_lists_to_test_strict", 
                        "background_genes_rigorous")
  
  missing_objs <- required_objects[!required_objects %in% ls()]
  if (length(missing_objs) > 0) {
    stop(paste("Missing required data objects:", paste(missing_objs, collapse = ", "), 
               "\nPlease run the 'Additional gene lists' analysis block first."))
  }

  message("Mapping individual genes to enrichment results...")

  # --- 2. Helper Function to Retrieve Overlapping Genes ---
  get_overlapping_genes <- function(ref_name, cond_name) {
    
    # A. Retrieve the Reference List (Disease Database)
    if (ref_name == "SFARI") {
      # Use the SFARI list defined in your environment
      ref_genes <- filtered_asd_gene_list_rna
    } else {
      # Use the lists loaded from the Excel file
      if (!exists("reference_gene_lists") || is.null(reference_gene_lists[[ref_name]])) {
        return(NA) # Safety check if list is missing
      }
      # Important: Re-apply the background filter to match the statistical test
      ref_genes <- intersect(reference_gene_lists[[ref_name]], background_genes_rigorous)
    }
    
    # B. Retrieve the Query List (Experimental Condition)
    query_genes <- gene_lists_to_test_strict[[cond_name]]
    
    # C. Find Intersection
    overlap <- intersect(ref_genes, query_genes)
    
    # D. Return as a comma-separated string
    if (length(overlap) > 0) {
      return(paste(overlap, collapse = ", "))
    } else {
      return("")
    }
  }

  # --- 3. Apply Function to the Dataframe ---
  # We iterate through the existing results table and calculate the genes for each row
  results_with_genes <- all_enrichment_data %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      Enriched_Genes = get_overlapping_genes(ReferenceList, Condition)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()

  # --- 4. Format for Excel ---
  # Rename columns to be "Publication Ready" and sort by P-value
  final_export_df <- results_with_genes %>%
    arrange(ReferenceList, PValue) %>%
    dplyr::select(
      Database = ReferenceList,
      Condition,
      Fold_Enrichment = FoldEnrichment,
      P_Value = PValue,
      NegLog10_PValue = Log10PValue,
      Overlap_Count = OverlapCount,
      Query_Size = QuerySize,
      Enriched_Genes # The new column with gene symbols
    )

  # --- 5. Split and Write to Excel ---
  # Split dataframe into a list of dataframes, one per Database
  sheets_list <- split(final_export_df, final_export_df$Database)
  
  output_filename <- "Disease_Database_Enrichment_With_Genes.xlsx"

  write.xlsx(
    sheets_list, 
    file = output_filename, 
    rowNames = FALSE,
    headerStyle = createStyle(textDecoration = "bold") # Optional: make headers bold
  )

  message(paste("Successfully exported:", output_filename))
  message(paste("Sheets created:", paste(names(sheets_list), collapse = ", ")))

}, error = function(e) {
  message("An error occurred during export: ", e$message)
})
```

```{r}
# --- Part 1: Prepare the Data Frames for Export ---

# Extract the full results table for the downregulated proteins
# The result is stored in the 'result' element of the g:profiler object
results_down <- as.data.frame(gostres_all_protein_down$result)

# Extract the full results table for the upregulated proteins
results_up <- as.data.frame(gostres_all_protein_up$result)

# Handle cases where one of the analyses might have found no significant terms
if (nrow(results_down) == 0) {
  results_down <- data.frame(Message = "No significant HPO terms were found for the downregulated protein set.")
}
if (nrow(results_up) == 0) {
  results_up <- data.frame(Message = "No significant HPO terms were found for the upregulated protein set.")
}

# --- Part 2: Write Both Data Frames to a Single Excel File ---

# Create a named list where each element is a data frame for a sheet
list_for_export <- list(
  "HPO_in_Downregulated_Proteins" = results_down,
  "HPO_in_Upregulated_Proteins" = results_up
)

# Write the list to an .xlsx file
write.xlsx(
  list_for_export, 
  file = "HPO_Enrichment_Results.xlsx",
  rowNames = FALSE
)

# Print a confirmation message
message("Successfully exported HPO enrichment results to 'HPO_Enrichment_Results.xlsx'")
```

14.13 Export of enriched transcriptions factors

```{r}
# --- Step 2: Combine All TF Result Lists ---
list_of_tf_lists <- list(
  "MGH_up"   = TF_list_MGH_RNA_up,
  "MGH_down" = TF_list_MGH_RNA_down,
  "8330_up"  = TF_list_8330_RNA_up,
  "8330_down"= TF_list_8330_RNA_down
)

# --- Step 3: Extract and Format Results for Each Sheet ---
all_tf_sheets <- list()

# Loop through the master list (e.g., "MGH_up", "8330_down")
for (list_name in names(list_of_tf_lists)) {
  
  current_tf_list <- list_of_tf_lists[[list_name]]
  
  # Loop through each individual comparison within that list
  for (comp_name in names(current_tf_list)) {
    
    sheet_name <- paste(list_name, comp_name, sep = "_")
    
    # Extract the results data frame
    res <- current_tf_list[[comp_name]]
    
    # Check if the result is valid and contains data
    if (!is.null(res) && nrow(res@result) > 0) {
      results_df <- as.data.frame(res@result)
    } else {
      results_df <- data.frame(Message = "No significant TF enrichment was found for this comparison.")
    }
    
    # Add the prepared data frame to our final export list
    all_tf_sheets[[sheet_name]] <- results_df
  }
}

# --- Step 4: Write the Combined List to a Single .xlsx File ---
write.xlsx(
  all_tf_sheets, 
  file = "TF_Enrichment_Results.xlsx",
  rowNames = FALSE,
  overwrite = TRUE
)

# --- Step 5: Print a Confirmation Message ---
message("Successfully exported all TF enrichment results to 'TF_Enrichment_Results.xlsx'")
message(paste("The file contains", length(all_tf_sheets), "sheets."))
```

14.15 Export Upset Plot intersections DEGs / DEPs / DEUs

```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# --- Shared Helper Function ---
# This function pads lists with NAs so they can be saved as columns in an Excel sheet
format_intersections_for_export <- function(intersection_list, name_map) {
  
  # Filter to only include intersections defined in our map
  list_to_process <- intersection_list[names(intersection_list) %in% names(name_map)]
  
  if (length(list_to_process) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(Message = "No intersections with the specified names were found."))
  }
  
  # Rename the list keys using the descriptive names
  names(list_to_process) <- name_map[names(list_to_process)]
  
  # Find max length for padding
  max_length <- max(sapply(list_to_process, length))
  if(max_length == 0) return(data.frame(Message = "Selected intersections are empty."))

  # Pad with NAs
  padded_list <- lapply(list_to_process, function(terms) {
    c(terms, rep(NA, max_length - length(terms)))
  })
  
  as.data.frame(padded_list)
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Part A: Export RNA (Genes)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Define Map (6 digits: DelC, DupC, HetDel, HomoDel, PWSTI, PWSTII)
intersection_name_map_rna <- c(
  "100000" = "DelC_specific",
  "010000" = "DupC_specific",
  "001000" = "HetDel_specific",
  "000100" = "HomoDel_specific",
  "000010" = "PWSTI_specific",
  "000001" = "PWSTII_specific",
  "110000" = "SYS_only (DelC_DupC)",
  "001100" = "MAGEL2_del_only (HetDel_HomoDel)",
  "000011" = "PWS_only (PWSTI_PWSTII)",
  "001111" = "All_Deletions (HetDel_HomoDel_PWSTI_PWSTII)",
  "011111" = "All_except_DelC",
  "111111" = "Shared_in_All"
)

# 2. Format Data
df_up_rna <- format_intersections_for_export(upregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection, intersection_name_map_rna)
df_down_rna <- format_intersections_for_export(downregulated_RNAonly_df_with_intersection, intersection_name_map_rna)

# 3. Write to Excel
write.xlsx(
  list("Upregulated_Genes" = df_up_rna, "Downregulated_Genes" = df_down_rna), 
  file = "Genes_Upset_Intersections_RNA_Named.xlsx"
)

# Part B: Export Protein (Proteins)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

  "001000" = "HetDel_Prot_specific",
  "000100" = "HomoDel_Prot_specific",
  "000010" = "PWSTI_Prot_specific",
  "000001" = "PWSTII_Prot_specific",
  "110000" = "SYS_Prot_only",
  "001100" = "MAGEL2_del_Prot_only",
  "000011" = "PWS_Prot_only",
  "001111" = "All_Deletions_Prot",
  "011111" = "All_except_DelC_Prot",

)

df_down_prot <- format_intersections_for_export(downregulated_Proteinonly_df_with_intersection, intersection_name_map_prot)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Part C: Export Ubiquitin (Proteins)

# the binary code is now 6 digits long, not 5.

  "100000" = "DelC_Ubi_specific",

  "111111" = "Shared_in_All_Ubi"
)

df_down_ubi <- format_intersections_for_export(downregulated_Ubionly_df_with_intersection, intersection_name_map_ubi)

)
```
