14.16 Export Upset Plot intersections DEGs / DEPs / DEUs combined Upset Plot

```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# --- Shared Helper Function (Included for completeness) ---
format_intersections_for_export <- function(intersection_list, name_map) {
  # Filter to only include intersections defined in our map
  list_to_process <- intersection_list[names(intersection_list) %in% names(name_map)]
  
  if (length(list_to_process) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(Message = "No intersections with the specified names were found."))
  }
  
  # Rename the list keys using the descriptive names
  names(list_to_process) <- name_map[names(list_to_process)]
  
  # Find max length for padding
  max_length <- max(sapply(list_to_process, length))
  if(max_length == 0) return(data.frame(Message = "Selected intersections are empty."))

  # Pad with NAs
  padded_list <- lapply(list_to_process, function(terms) {
    c(terms, rep(NA, max_length - length(terms)))
  })
  
  as.data.frame(padded_list, check.names = FALSE)
}


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Define Mapping for Multi-Omics (17 digits)
# Structure: 6 RNA | 6 Protein | 5 Ubiquitin
# ---------------------------------------------------------

intersection_name_map_multi <- c(
  # The "Grand" Intersection (Everything overlapping in all datasets)
  "11111111111111111" = "Shared_in_All_Layers (RNA+Prot+Ubi)",

  # Layer-Specific "All" Intersections
  "11111100000000000" = "Shared_RNA_Only (All Genotypes)",
  "00000011111100000" = "Shared_Protein_Only (All Genotypes)",
  "00000000000011111" = "Shared_Ubiquitin_Only (All Genotypes)",

  # Cross-Layer Intersections
  "11111111111100000" = "Shared_RNA_and_Protein (No Ubi)",
  "00000011111111111" = "Shared_Protein_and_Ubiquitin (No RNA)"
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Apply Formatting
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Format Upregulated Multi-Omics
df_up_multi <- format_intersections_for_export(
  upregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection, 
  intersection_name_map_multi
)

# 2. Format Downregulated Multi-Omics
df_down_multi <- format_intersections_for_export(
  downregulated_RNA_prot_ubi_df_with_intersection, 
  intersection_name_map_multi
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Write to Excel
# ---------------------------------------------------------

write.xlsx(
  list(
    "Upregulated_MultiOmics" = df_up_multi, 
    "Downregulated_MultiOmics" = df_down_multi
  ), 
  file = "Genes_Upset_Intersections_MultiOmics_Named.xlsx"
)

message("Successfully exported Multi-Omics intersections to 'Genes_Upset_Intersections_MultiOmics_Named.xlsx'")
```

14.17 Export Upset Plot intersections DEGs / DEPs / DEUs mixed Upset Plot

```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# --- Shared Helper Function ---
format_intersections_for_export <- function(intersection_list, name_map) {
  # Filter to only include intersections defined in our map
  # (and check if they actually exist in the data to avoid errors)
  valid_names <- names(name_map)[names(name_map) %in% names(intersection_list)]
  
  if (length(valid_names) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(Message = "No intersections with the specified names were found."))
  }
  
  # Subset and Rename
  list_to_process <- intersection_list[valid_names]
  names(list_to_process) <- name_map[valid_names]
  
  # Handle empty lists inside the subset
  if (length(list_to_process) == 0) return(data.frame(Message = "Selected intersections are empty."))
  
  # Find max length for padding
  max_length <- max(sapply(list_to_process, length))
  if(max_length == 0) return(data.frame(Message = "Selected intersections contain no genes."))
  
  # Pad with NAs to make a rectangular dataframe
  padded_list <- lapply(list_to_process, function(terms) {
    c(terms, rep(NA, max_length - length(terms)))
  })
  
  as.data.frame(padded_list, check.names = FALSE)
}


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Define Mapping for Mixed Multi-Omics (17 digits)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
intersection_name_map_mixed <- c(
  # 1. The "Grand" Intersection
  "11111111111111111" = "Overlap_All_3_Layers",

  # 2. Pairwise Overlaps
  "11111111111100000" = "Overlap_RNA_and_Protein",
  "00000011111111111" = "Overlap_Protein_and_Ubiquitin",
  "11111100000011111" = "Overlap_RNA_and_Ubiquitin",

  # 3. Single Layer Exclusives (Strictly specific to that layer in this context)
  "11111100000000000" = "Exclusive_RNA",
  "00000011111100000" = "Exclusive_Protein",
  "00000000000011111" = "Exclusive_Ubiquitin"
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Apply Formatting to All 6 Scenarios
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# Note: These objects (df_..._isect) must be created by running the previous code blocks.

# 1. T(Down) P(Down) U(Up)
df_export_TD_PD_UU <- format_intersections_for_export(df_TD_PD_UU_isect, intersection_name_map_mixed)

# 2. T(Down) P(Up) U(Down)
df_export_TD_PU_UD <- format_intersections_for_export(df_TD_PU_UD_isect, intersection_name_map_mixed)

# 3. T(Down) P(Up) U(Up)
df_export_TD_PU_UU <- format_intersections_for_export(df_TD_PU_UU_isect, intersection_name_map_mixed)

# 4. T(Up) P(Down) U(Down)
df_export_TU_PD_UD <- format_intersections_for_export(df_TU_PD_UD_isect, intersection_name_map_mixed)

# 5. T(Up) P(Up) U(Down)
df_export_TU_PU_UD <- format_intersections_for_export(df_TU_PU_UD_isect, intersection_name_map_mixed)

# 6. T(Up) P(Down) U(Up)
df_export_TU_PD_UU <- format_intersections_for_export(df_TU_PD_UU_isect, intersection_name_map_mixed)


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Write to Excel (One Sheet Per Plot)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

write.xlsx(
  list(
    "TD_PD_UU" = df_export_TD_PD_UU,
    "TD_PU_UD" = df_export_TD_PU_UD,
    "TD_PU_UU" = df_export_TD_PU_UU,
    "TU_PD_UD" = df_export_TU_PD_UD,
    "TU_PU_UD" = df_export_TU_PU_UD,
    "TU_PD_UU" = df_export_TU_PD_UU
  ),
  file = "Genes_Upset_Intersections_Mixed_Scenarios.xlsx"
)

message("Successfully exported 6 mixed scenarios to 'Genes_Upset_Intersections_Mixed_Scenarios.xlsx'")
```

14.18 Export of DEGs, DEPs and DEUs shared across all variants and backgrounds

```{r}

# --- Helper Function to Combine Unequal Lists ---
# This function ensures that if 'Down' has 5 items and 'Up' has 100, 
# we don't get an error. It fills the empty spots with NA.
combine_lists_to_df <- function(down_list, up_list, col_name_prefix) {
  # 1. Determine the maximum length between the two lists
  max_len <- max(length(down_list), length(up_list))
  
  # 2. Pad the lists with NA so they are the same length
  # (If the list is shorter than max_len, fill the rest with NA)
  down_padded <- c(down_list, rep(NA, max_len - length(down_list)))
  up_padded   <- c(up_list,   rep(NA, max_len - length(up_list)))
  
  # 3. Create the data frame
  df <- data.frame(Downregulated = down_padded, Upregulated = up_padded)
  
  return(df)
}

# --- Prepare the Data Frames ---

# 1. Transcriptome Sheet Data
df_transcriptome <- combine_lists_to_df(intersection_RNA_down, intersection_RNA_up)

# 2. Proteome Sheet Data
df_proteome <- combine_lists_to_df(intersection_proteins_down, intersection_proteins_up)

# 3. Ubiquitome Sheet Data
df_ubiquitome <- combine_lists_to_df(intersection_Ubi_down, intersection_Ubi_up)

# --- Create the List of Sheets ---
# The names in this list (e.g., "Transcriptome") will become the Excel Sheet names
sheets_list <- list(
  "Transcriptome" = df_transcriptome,
  "Proteome"      = df_proteome,
  "Ubiquitome"    = df_ubiquitome
)

# --- Export to Excel ---
write.xlsx(sheets_list, file = "Omics_Intersections_Summary.xlsx", rowNames = FALSE)

message("Export complete: Omics_Intersections_Summary.xlsx")
```

15.1 Alternative to semantic GO term clustering

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr) # Required for wrapping text

# ==============================================================================
# HELPER 1: Calculate Geometric Mean Ranking (Unchanged)
# ==============================================================================
calculate_geomean_ranking <- function(terms_of_interest, list_of_enrich_objects, pattern_to_use = ".") {
  
  if (length(terms_of_interest) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  all_experiments <- names(list_of_enrich_objects)
  relevant_experiments <- grep(pattern_to_use, all_experiments, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
  
  if (length(relevant_experiments) == 0) {
    message(paste("Warning: No experiments matched pattern:", pattern_to_use))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  p_matrix <- matrix(1.0, nrow = length(terms_of_interest), ncol = length(relevant_experiments))
  rownames(p_matrix) <- terms_of_interest
  colnames(p_matrix) <- relevant_experiments
  
  for (exp_name in relevant_experiments) {
    res_obj <- list_of_enrich_objects[[exp_name]]
    if (!is.null(res_obj) && nrow(res_obj@result) > 0) {
      res_df <- res_obj@result
      matches <- match(terms_of_interest, res_df$Description)
      valid_indices <- !is.na(matches)
      if (any(valid_indices)) {
        matched_pvals <- res_df$p.adjust[matches[valid_indices]]
        matched_pvals[is.na(matched_pvals)] <- 1.0
        p_matrix[valid_indices, exp_name] <- matched_pvals
      }
    }
  }
  
  geo_means <- apply(p_matrix, 1, function(x) exp(mean(log(x + 1e-300))))
  
  ranking_df <- data.frame(
    Term = names(geo_means),
    GeoMeanP = geo_means,
    NegLog10P = -log10(geo_means)
  )
  
  ranking_df <- ranking_df[order(ranking_df$NegLog10P, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  return(ranking_df)
}

# ==============================================================================
# HELPER 2: Determine Regex Pattern (Unchanged)
# ==============================================================================
get_pattern_from_name <- function(set_name) {
  if (grepl("RNA_plus_Ubi", set_name)) return("RNA|Ubi")
  if (grepl("RNA_plus_Prot", set_name)) return("RNA|Protein")
  if (grepl("Ubi_plus_Prot", set_name)) return("Ubi|Protein")
  if (grepl("all_RNA", set_name)) return("RNA")
  if (grepl("all_Protein", set_name)) return("Protein")
  if (grepl("all_Ubi", set_name)) return("Ubi")
  if (grepl("DelC-exclusive|except_DelC", set_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) return("DupC|HetDel|HomoDel|PWSTI|PWSTII")
  if (grepl("PWS", set_name)) return("PWSTI|PWSTII")
  if (grepl("MAGEL2", set_name)) return("HetDel|HomoDel") 
  if (grepl("DupC", set_name)) return("DupC")
  if (grepl("DelC", set_name)) return("DelC") 
  if (grepl("All", set_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) return(".") 
  return(".") 
}

# ==============================================================================
# HELPER 3: Visualize Top 10 Terms (UPDATED WITH TEXT WRAPPING)
# ==============================================================================
plot_top10_bar <- function(ranking_df, plot_title, bar_color = "firebrick") {
  
  if (is.null(ranking_df) || nrow(ranking_df) == 0) {
    message(paste("Skipping plot for:", plot_title, "(No data)"))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Select Top 10
  n_terms <- min(10, nrow(ranking_df))
  top_n <- head(ranking_df, n_terms)
  
  # --- THE FIX: Wrap long text at 45 characters ---
  # This prevents the label from pushing the plot area to the right
  top_n$TermWrapped <- str_wrap(top_n$Term, width = 45)
  
  p <- ggplot(top_n, aes(x = NegLog10P, y = reorder(TermWrapped, NegLog10P))) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = bar_color, width = 0.7) +
    
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      subtitle = paste("Top", n_terms, "Representative GO Terms"),
      x = "-Log10 (Geometric Mean P-adj)",
      y = ""
    ) +
    
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
      # Adjust lineheight so wrapped text looks neat
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, lineheight = 0.8), 
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(p)
}
```

15.2 GO term ranking transcriptome

```{r}
# --- Prepare Source Lists for RNA ---
sources_RNA_UP <- c(GO_list_MGH_RNA_up, GO_list_8330_RNA_up)
sources_RNA_DOWN <- c(GO_list_MGH_RNA_down, GO_list_8330_RNA_down)

message("--- Ranking and Plotting RNA GO Intersections (Bar Plots - Wrapped Labels) ---")

for (set_name in names(list_of_go_sets)) {
  
  # Check direction strictly
  if (grepl("^Up", set_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    current_sources <- sources_RNA_UP
    color_theme <- "#D55E00" 
  } else {
    current_sources <- sources_RNA_DOWN
    color_theme <- "#0072B2" 
  }
  
  pattern <- get_pattern_from_name(set_name)
  terms <- list_of_go_sets[[set_name]]
  
  ranked_res <- calculate_geomean_ranking(terms, current_sources, pattern_to_use = pattern)
  
  if (!is.null(ranked_res)) {
    p <- plot_top10_bar(ranked_res, paste("RNA:", set_name), color_theme)
    print(p)
  }
}
```

# --- 2. Iterate through the set list ---
# Note: 'protein_go_sets' is the list defined in your section 7.8
for (set_name in names(protein_go_sets)) {
  
  # --- STRICT DIRECTION CHECK (Using ^Up) ---
  if (grepl("^Up", set_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    current_sources <- sources_Prot_UP
    color_theme <- "#D55E00" # Orange/Red
  } else {
    current_sources <- sources_Prot_DOWN
    color_theme <- "#0072B2" # Blue
  }
  
  # Determine Pattern (e.g., "DupC" or "PWSTI|PWSTII")
  pattern <- get_pattern_from_name(set_name)
  
  # Get Terms
  terms <- protein_go_sets[[set_name]]
  
  # Calculate Ranking
  ranked_res <- calculate_geomean_ranking(terms, current_sources, pattern_to_use = pattern)
  
  # Plot with Text Wrapping
  if (!is.null(ranked_res)) {
    p <- plot_top10_bar(ranked_res, paste("Protein:", set_name), color_theme)
    print(p)
  }
}

15.4 GO term ranking ubiquitome

```{r}
# --- 1. Prepare Source Lists for Ubiquitin ---
sources_Ubi_UP <- c(GO_list_MGH_Ubi_up, GO_list_8330_Ubi_up)
sources_Ubi_DOWN <- c(GO_list_MGH_Ubi_down, GO_list_8330_Ubi_down)

message("--- Ranking and Plotting Ubiquitin GO Intersections (Bar Plots) ---")

for (set_name in names(ubi_go_sets)) {
  
  # Direction Check
  if (grepl("^Up", set_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    current_sources <- sources_Ubi_UP
    color_theme <- "#D55E00" 
  } else {
    current_sources <- sources_Ubi_DOWN
    color_theme <- "#0072B2" 
  }
  
  # Get Base Pattern
  pattern <- get_pattern_from_name(set_name)
  
  # --- PATCH: Fix for "All Ubiquitin" ---
  # Your Ubiquitin lists are named "Protein_...", so searching for "Ubi" fails.
  # Since 'current_sources' ONLY contains Ubiquitin data here, we can safely 
  # select "." (all columns) for the "all_Ubi" set.
  if (grepl("all_Ubi", set_name)) {
    pattern <- "."
  }
  
  terms <- ubi_go_sets[[set_name]]
  
  ranked_res <- calculate_geomean_ranking(terms, current_sources, pattern_to_use = pattern)
  
  if (!is.null(ranked_res)) {
    p <- plot_top10_bar(ranked_res, paste("Ubiquitin:", set_name), color_theme)
    print(p)
  }
}
```

15.5 GO term ranking combined

```{r}
# --- 1. Prepare Combined Source Lists ---
sources_ALL_UP <- c(
  GO_list_MGH_RNA_up, GO_list_8330_RNA_up,
  GO_list_MGH_Protein_up, GO_list_8330_Protein_up,
  GO_list_MGH_Ubi_up, GO_list_8330_Ubi_up
)

sources_ALL_DOWN <- c(
  GO_list_MGH_RNA_down, GO_list_8330_RNA_down,
  GO_list_MGH_Protein_down, GO_list_8330_Protein_down,
  GO_list_MGH_Ubi_down, GO_list_8330_Ubi_down
)

message("\n--- Ranking and Plotting Multi-Omics GO Intersections (Bar Plots - Wrapped Labels) ---")

# --- 2. Iterate through the set list ---
# Note: 'combined_go_sets' is the list defined in your section 7.18
for (set_name in names(combined_go_sets)) {
  
  # --- STRICT DIRECTION CHECK (Using ^Up) ---
  if (grepl("^Up", set_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    current_sources <- sources_ALL_UP
    color_theme <- "#D55E00" # Orange/Red
  } else {
    current_sources <- sources_ALL_DOWN
    color_theme <- "#0072B2" # Blue
  }
  
  # Determine Pattern (Uses the layer-specific logic in get_pattern_from_name)
  pattern <- get_pattern_from_name(set_name)
  
  # Get Terms
  terms <- combined_go_sets[[set_name]]
  
  # Calculate Ranking
  ranked_res <- calculate_geomean_ranking(terms, current_sources, pattern_to_use = pattern)
  
  # Plot with Text Wrapping
  if (!is.null(ranked_res)) {
    p <- plot_top10_bar(ranked_res, paste("Multi-Omics:", set_name), color_theme)
    print(p)
  }
}
```
